=== constants 280-320 ===
  # Ubuntu CD Image Automatic Signing Key (2012, RSA4096), cdimage@ubuntu.com —
  # signs cdimage SHA256SUMS.gpg. The trust anchor: tiny, stable, auditable. The
  # cloud step fetches the key BY this fingerprint into a clean keyring, so the
  # keyserver is never trusted — only a signature from exactly this key passes.
  readonly RBGC_LODE_WSL_SIGNING_FPR="843938DF228D22F7B3742BC0D94AA3F0EFE21092"

  # podvm-kind acquisition convention — NOT resolved coordinates (see RBSLI). Per
  # the no-FQIN premise, intent stays declarative and the pipeline resolves leaf
  # digests at capture: immure takes the quay FAMILY and the podman VERSION as
  # arguments (e.g. `podvm-wsl 5.6`); the cloud select step reads the family's
  # multi-arch OCI index at that version and picks the curated {disktype × arch}
  # leaves below from the index child DESCRIPTOR's platform.architecture +
  # annotations.disktype — never the layer filename, which is unreliable (the 5.6
  # wsl x86_64 leaf is titled `5.0-rootfs-amd64.tar.zst`; memo-20260608 §3.4). No
  # digest is pinned; advancing the version is a different argument, not a constant
  # edit. Trust grade is recorded-at-acquisition — quay rotates podvm out within
  # days and publishes no durable checksum, so RB attests only the digest captured.
  #
  # Two quay families, one verb (immure spans both via the family argument). The
  # disktype leaves carry the ALT arch spelling (x86_64/aarch64), not the OCI
  # amd64/arm64 the plain container children use — selection keys on that spelling.
  readonly RBGC_LODE_PODVM_FAMILY_WSL="quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl"
  readonly RBGC_LODE_PODVM_FAMILY_NATIVE="quay.io/podman/machine-os"
  # Curated leaf selection per family — declarative `disktype:arch` rows the select
  # step matches against index child descriptors. THIS PACE proves the podvm-wsl
  # family end-to-end (both wsl-disktype leaves -> a genuine 2-member Lode, which
  # exercises the multi-member machinery and the per-member-jettison path). The
  # native family's full 8-leaf curation and a same-version "widen the set" refresh
  # mode are the FOLLOWING pace (RBSL "Why podvm kinds retain selectively"); the
  # native default below is the modest 2-leaf set the cerebro experiment captured
  # (memo-20260608 §5) — the verb works for native, but only podvm-wsl is fixture-
  # proven. Member tag composes as :<sprue><disktype>-<arch> (e.g. rbi_wsl-x86_64);
  # disktype+arch are RB-selected from the index, so sprued.
  readonly RBGC_LODE_PODVM_WSL_SELECTION="wsl:x86_64 wsl:aarch64"
  readonly RBGC_LODE_PODVM_NATIVE_SELECTION="qemu:x86_64 wsl:x86_64"

  # rbi_df layout: flat namespace. No subdirs. Each filename names one
  # depot-scoped artifact; tag varies by artifact role.
  #
  # Current artifacts:
  #   probe-tether:probe   — tether pool levy-time capability probe

=== gcrane-append snippet ===
#!/bin/bash
# RBGJS gcrane-append — push a prepared directory as a FROM-scratch single-layer OCI
# image via `gcrane append --oci-empty-base`. The Lode-family replacement for the
# buildx-bootstrap + buildx-push pair: gcrane needs no builder bootstrap and no daemon.
# Forked deliberately rather than converting buildx-push in place — that snippet is
# still @rbgjs_include'd by the made-side multi-platform hallmark vouch (rbgjv03),
# outside the Lode family and out of this scope. Single-platform by construction (one
# appended layer over an empty OCI base). Idempotent under retry (CBi_103): re-appending
# identical bytes to the same tag yields an identical digest.
#   requires: APPEND_CTX  dir whose entire contents become the single layer
#                         (files land at image root, matching buildx `COPY x /`)
#             APPEND_URI  full destination image ref including tag
#   provides: the FROM-scratch image pushed to APPEND_URI
APPEND_TAR="${APPEND_CTX%/}.layer.tar"
tar -C "${APPEND_CTX}" -cf "${APPEND_TAR}" . \
  || { echo "FATAL: failed to tar layer from ${APPEND_CTX}" >&2; exit 1; }
gcrane append --oci-empty-base -f "${APPEND_TAR}" -t "${APPEND_URI}" \
  || { echo "FATAL: gcrane append failed for ${APPEND_URI}" >&2; exit 1; }

rbld_augur() {
  zrbld_sentinel

  local -r z_touchmark="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"

  buc_doc_brief "Augur a Lode — inspect member tags and decode its rbi_vouch provenance envelope (read-only)"
  buc_doc_param "touchmark" "Lode stamp to inspect (e.g., b260602120000)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${z_touchmark}" || buc_die "Touchmark parameter required"
  [[ "${z_touchmark}" =~ ^[a-z]+[0-9]{12}$ ]] \
    || buc_die "Touchmark format invalid: '${z_touchmark}' (expected <kind><YYMMDDHHMMSS>, e.g. b260602120000)"

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  test -f "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" \
    || buc_die "Director credential not found: ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
  local z_token=""
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"

  local -r z_pkg="${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_touchmark}"
  local -r z_pkg_encoded="${z_pkg//\//%2F}"

  # Member tags — every tag on the Lode package. An empty package is the
  # "Lode not present" signal (matches divine's enumerate contract).
  buc_step "Fetching member tags for Lode ${z_pkg}"
  local -r z_tags_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg_encoded}/tags?pageSize=1000"
  local -r z_tags_infix="rbld_augur_tags"
  rbuh_json "GET" "${z_tags_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_tags_infix}"
  rbuh_require_ok "List tags for Lode ${z_touchmark}" "${z_tags_infix}"

  local -r z_resp_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_tags_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"
  local -r z_tags_file="${ZRBLD_AUGUR_PREFIX}tags.txt"
  jq -r '.tags[]?.name | sub(".*/tags/"; "")' "${z_resp_file}" > "${z_tags_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to extract member tags for Lode ${z_touchmark}"
  test -s "${z_tags_file}" \
    || buc_die "No member tags found under ${z_pkg} — Lode not present in registry"

  local z_tags=()
  local z_tag=""
  while IFS= read -r z_tag || test -n "${z_tag}"; do
    test -n "${z_tag}" || continue
    z_tags+=("${z_tag}")
  done < "${z_tags_file}"

  echo ""
  printf "  Member tags (%s):\n" "${#z_tags[@]}"
  for z_tag in "${z_tags[@]}"; do
    printf "    %s\n" "${z_tag}"
  done

  # Provenance envelope — extract the :rbi_vouch FROM-scratch artifact and read
  # its vouch.json layer. This is the inspect depth divine never carried: the
  # acquisition facts and the honest trust-grade posture. The same gcrane-pushed
  # single-layer image rbgjl02 wrote (vouch.json at image root).
  buc_step "Decoding provenance envelope (:${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH})"
  local -r z_vouch_dir="${ZRBLD_AUGUR_PREFIX}vouch"
  rm -rf "${z_vouch_dir}" || buc_die "Failed to clear vouch scratch dir: ${z_vouch_dir}"
  zrbfc_gar_extract_artifact "${z_token}" "${z_pkg}" "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH}" "${z_vouch_dir}" \
    || buc_die "No :${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH} envelope at ${z_pkg} — not a vouched Lode (capture incomplete or a legacy artifact)"
  local -r z_vouch_json="${z_vouch_dir}/vouch.json"
  test -f "${z_vouch_json}" \
    || buc_die "Envelope artifact present but vouch.json missing for ${z_touchmark}"

  # Kind name comes from the envelope's own kind field — the kind-agnostic source
  # (a new Lode kind needs no change here; the prefix letter is shown as a cross-check).
  local z_trust=""
  z_trust=$(jq -r '.trust_grade // "(absent)"' "${z_vouch_json}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to read trust_grade from envelope for ${z_touchmark}"

  echo ""
  printf "  Provenance envelope (:%s):\n" "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH}"
  jq -r '
    "    Kind:          \(.kind // "(absent)")",
    "    Lode:          \(.lode // "(absent)")",
    "    Schema:        \(.schema // "(absent)")",
    "    Acquired at:   \(.acquired_at // "(absent)")",
    "    Acquired by:   \(.acquired_by // "(absent)")",
    "    Capture build: \(.capture_build // "(absent)")",
    "    Signature:     \(if .signature == null then "(unsigned)" else .signature end)",

content.text:
Both questions close cleanly. `augur` reads `:rbi_vouch` by tag through a fresh GAR fetch (`zrbfc_gar_extract_artifact`), and `gcrane append -t` re-points that tag — so a refresh that re-pushes the envelope is read cleanly, no caching, no augur change needed. And augur's touchmark regex already accepts the two-letter `vn`/`vw` prefix.

Here's where I land.

## Scope — Fable was exact
Family dispatch landed end-to-end in ₢BHAAW. This pace is three things: **(a)** swap the native selection constant from the 2-leaf cerebro default to the full 8-leaf curation (a one-line constant); **(b)** refresh mode — the only new machinery; **(c)** proof. (a) and (c) are mechanical; (b) is the design.

## Refresh design — and I diverge from the idempotency lean
Fable's hypothesis was that gcrane-cp idempotency lets refresh re-emit the *full* widened set with no skip-list. That holds for the **blob copy** but breaks on the **defensive premise** that is the entire reason podvm capture exists: *don't depend on a hostile, rotating upstream.* If refresh re-resolves the originals from the live index, then within quay's rotation window it can either silently overwrite safely-captured bytes with rotated ones, or **die** when an original digest has already aged out — coupling bytes you already hold to upstream's continued cooperation, which is the exact dependency the Lode severs.

So the honest refresh is **add-only / preserve-originals**:
- Resolve and capture only `widened_selection − already_captured`; leave the originals' captured digests untouched.
- That forces **per-member `acquired_at` + `capture_build`** in the envelope (originals copied verbatim keep their true capture time; new members stamp NOW) — which is the resolution to the exact "whole-envelope lies about the originals" question Fable flagged. Whole-envelope `acquired_at`/`capture_build` redefine to "envelope last authored," which augur already displays correctly as-is.
- "Same version, not a bump" gets enforced *structurally*: refresh takes no version argument — it reads the target Lode's own version from its envelope and locks to it. You can't bump what you can't pass.

The skip-list earns its place not for avoiding redundant copies (idempotency already handles that) but for **never re-touching the hostile upstream for bytes you already hold.** That's load-bearing, not gold-plating.

**4KB slot:** not tight — 8 members with per-member times and real origin strings is ~2.5 KB, comfortably under. The forensic envelope-in-slot stays.

## The one decision that's yours — fixture spend
A full-8-leaf native case pulls multi-GB from quay on **every service-suite run, forever**. Two defensible shapes:

- **Lean recurring + one-time gate (my lean):** the recurring `podvm-lifecycle` case proves fan-out + refresh at *minimal* curation (2–3 leaves, both families); full-8-leaf native capture is this pace's **one-time live gate**, run once at mount and recorded, not baked into the suite. Keeps `service` cheap forever; full curation still proven once.
- **Full curation recurring:** the suite proves the real curated set every run. Maximal fidelity, recurring multi-GB cost.

Which way on the fixture? And with that settled, I'll design the per-member envelope schema in full, then delegate the constant + body mechanics + fixture cases to sonnet and verify before the live run — unless you want to see the full envelope schema and the host-vs-cloud-step "read existing members" sub-choice before I hand anything off.

lastPrompt: On ₢BHAAL approach (from Fable, who reviewed the landed immure body pre-wrap):    Yes, pull the body first — and here's what you'll find: the extension surface is smaller than the docket   implies. ₢B…

content: Mounting pace ₢BHAAL on heat ₣BH: adding podvm refresh mode to the immure capture verb. I've designed it (add-only, preserve originals, per-member envelope timestamps) and need one decision from you: should the recurring fixture prove full 8-leaf native curation, or stay lean with full curation as a one-time gate?

message.content:
For ₢BHAAL (from Fable): Your add-only refresh is correct and I withdraw the idempotent re-emit lean —
  re-resolution can return rebuilt leaves under the same version tag, and the skip-list is what keeps captured
  member tags from silently repointing. That's load-bearing, exactly as you argued.

  Fixture spend — lean recurring + one-time gate, with one refinement. Don't build a fixture-special lean native
  selection: that needs either a test seam or divergence from the shipped constant, both worse than the
  problem. Instead, three tiers, no new machinery:

  1. Recurring service: the podvm-lifecycle fixture proves the machinery — fan-out dispatch and the refresh path
  — on the wsl family, whose production curation is already lean (2 leaves). No override channel needed; the
  fixture exercises shipped constants.
  2. Fast tier: add a host-side case asserting zrbld_immure_resolve_family resolves both brands to their
  (kind-letter, repo, selection) tuples — it's a pure bash function, so the native resolve-arm gets regression
  cover for free, no cloud, no cost. This closes the "native arm silently rots" gap that gate-only coverage
  would leave.
  3. One-time gate: full-8-leaf native capture runs live once at this pace's verification and is recorded in the
  wrap — never baked into the suite.

  Yes — show the envelope schema and the read-existing-members sub-choice before delegation. Two items to settle
  in that same screen:

  - The crash-resume rule. Vouch pushes last, so a refresh can crash between member-cp and envelope re-push,
  leaving captured tags the envelope doesn't name. Define the skip-list's source of truth (GAR tag presence vs
  envelope members) and how an orphaned tag gets enveloped on the next refresh — including what acquired_at
  honestly says for a member whose true capture time was lost in the crashed run.
  - The schema judgment. Per-member acquired_at/capture_build changes the members[] shape that augur and any
  future verifier consume. Decide whether it rides rbld-vouch-1 as optional additions or bumps the schema
  constant — and state explicitly that other kinds don't adopt per-member times (only podvm has refresh, so the
  asymmetry is load-bearing; say so where the schema is defined, or the next uniformity sweep will "fix" it).

  On the sub-choice itself, one lean to weigh rather than a verdict: computing already-captured in-pool (the
  select step reads the target Lode's tags with a metadata token, rbgjl06 precedent) keeps capture logic
  cloud-side and the host declarative; a host-computed skip-list passed as a substitution splits the logic
  across the seam. Your call in the design.

  4KB arithmetic accepted; envelope-in-slot stays.

  fields). Bump the schema value to rbld-vouch-2. No dual-dialect reading — pre-MVP, no compatibility surface:
  augur reads v2 only, and the standing bole Lodes get re-ensconced (depot is disposable). The buildStepOutputs
  result format (slot_1/stamp/vouch) is also ours on both sides — mint it its own sprue per the minting
  discipline and surface that mint alongside the schema for blessing; do not reuse rblv_ for it.

  Recurring service: podvm-lifecycle proves fan-out + refresh on the wsl family (its production curation is

  after the schema is blessed.

content.content:
=== ACG 128-150 ===
lens `**Gestalt**:` field is the blessed form: metaphor marked as interpretive
guidance). Unmarked ornament is contamination: it spends reader attention and
implies constraint where none exists. A code comment restating design-time
knowledge is the same failure in the other medium (temporal misallocation,
above); flowery spec prose is its mirror.

## The fourth clause — wire formats are named homes

A JSON document this project authors on **both sides** — an envelope, a result
slot, a substitutions blob — is itself a home: a wire format with senders and
receivers that must stay findable from each other. Its keys are therefore
**minted names** (CLAUDE.md mint: any persistent name anywhere is in scope),
carrying one key-sprue per format: `rblv_digest`, never bare `digest`. The sprue
is what makes the format greppable — `grep rblv_` returns the author, every
consumer, and the schema definition, with no central registry to drift (the
`buo` tweak-sprue precedent; the `_RBGL_` substitution registers are the senior
worked example). One sprue per *format*, not per file or per domain: two formats
sharing a sprue blur the census that is the sprue's whole purpose.

The boundary is the Palisade: JSON conforming to a **foreign** schema — a Cloud
Build envelope, an OCI manifest, a vendor API response — keeps the foreign keys.
Sprued keys inside a foreign document are the tell that someone is decorating a
schema they don't own.
=== ACG 330-345 ===
- **Licensing:** mutate-now when the format's author and every consumer convert
  in one move and a live verifier covers the path — a rename either resolves
  everywhere or the suite fails loud. Detect-only when the consumers cannot all
  convert together; for an RB-authored format that inability is itself a finding
  worth recording.
- **Verifier:** the consuming suite tier for the format (e.g. the capture
  fixtures for the Lode vouch envelope), plus the census check: `grep <sprue>_`
  returns exactly the format's sites and nothing else.
- **Done:** every key in the format carries the sprue; the format's schema
  identifier (if it has one) is bumped; the sprue grep is the format's complete
  sender/receiver census. First worked application: the Lode vouch envelope
  (`rblv_`, schema `rbld-vouch-2`).
- **Triage:** foreign-schema documents are excluded by the Palisade boundary
  before any sweep. Pre-MVP formats convert with no dual-dialect reading — there
  is no compatibility surface; standing artifacts are re-captured or discarded,
  never grandfathered.

=== spine extract: agnostic or slot-aware? ===
zrbld_spine_extract() {
  zrbfc_sentinel

  local -r z_step_index="${1:?Step index required}"
  local -r z_dest_file="${2:?Destination file required}"
  local -r z_b64_file="${z_dest_file}.b64"

  jq -r ".results.buildStepOutputs[${z_step_index}] // empty" "${ZRBFC_BUILD_STATUS_FILE}" \
    > "${z_b64_file}" || buc_die "Failed to extract buildStepOutputs[${z_step_index}] from build result"
  test -s "${z_b64_file}" || buc_die "No buildStepOutputs[${z_step_index}] in build result — step produced no output"

  rbgo_base64_decode_file_to_file "${z_b64_file}" "${z_dest_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to decode buildStepOutputs[${z_step_index}] base64"
  test -s "${z_dest_file}" || buc_die "Empty decoded buildStepOutputs[${z_step_index}]"
}

Here's the v2 schema screen. One mint needs your blessing — the slot format's sprue; `rblv_` you've already pinned in ACG/CBG so I'm just applying it.

## 1 · Vouch envelope — `rblv_`, schema `rbld-vouch-2`

```json
{
  "rblv_schema":        "rbld-vouch-2",
  "rblv_kind":          "podvm-native",
  "rblv_lode":          "vn260610095327",
  "rblv_acquired_at":   "2026-06-10T09:53:27Z",      // envelope authored-at (this/last authoring build)
  "rblv_acquired_by":   "<mason SA email>",
  "rblv_capture_build": "<build id of this authoring>",
  "rblv_trust_grade":   "recorded",
  "rblv_signature":     null,
  "rblv_members": [
    {
      "rblv_name":          "rbi_qemu-x86_64",
      "rblv_origin":        "quay.io/podman/machine-os:5.6",
      "rblv_digest":        "sha256:…",
      "rblv_verification":  "recorded",
      "rblv_tags":          ["rbi_qemu-x86_64"],
      "rblv_acquired_at":   "2026-06-10T09:53:27Z",   // PODVM-ONLY — this leaf's true capture time
      "rblv_capture_build": "<build id that captured this leaf>"  // PODVM-ONLY
    }
  ]
}
```

Envelope-level `rblv_acquired_at`/`rblv_capture_build` mean **"when this envelope was authored"** uniformly across all kinds. For single-capture kinds (bole/reliquary/wsl) authoring coincides with capture, so the member-level twins are **omitted** — the envelope time already is the leaf time. Refresh is the only thing that splits authoring from capture, so only podvm carries member-level times. No top-level `rblv_version` — refresh reads the locked version from any member's `rblv_origin` (see §3).

## 2 · Slot/result format — needs a sprue (proposing `rbls_`)

This is the buildStepOutputs payload every capture step writes and every body's extract reads — a distinct format from the envelope, so ACG's fourth clause ("one sprue per format") forbids reusing `rblv_`. Grep gate: `rbls` is clean (0 hits).

```json
{
  "rbls_slot_1": {
    "rbls_stamp": "vn260610095327",
    "rbls_vouch": { …the rblv_ envelope… }     // layered: each format its own sprue
  }
}
```

**Blessing ask** — `rbls_` (Lode Slot), terminal child of `rbl`, sibling to `rblv_`. Two soft spots I'd rather you weigh than I bury: (a) `rbls_slot_1` carries a mild "slot" redundancy — I could drop the outer noun to `rbls_1` but it reads more cryptic; (b) `rbls_` sits one char off `rblds_` (the spine code prefix) — grep-clean (`rbls_` never matches `rblds_`) but visually close. Both are why this is yours to pin, not mine. Say the word and I apply whatever you bless.

## 3 · Crash-resume rule (refresh's skip-list)

Refresh interface: `immure <family> --refresh <touchmark>` — no version argument (structurally can't bump; version is derived from the target's envelope). Fresh capture is the empty-target case of the same path.

**Source of truth: the GAR member tags — never the envelope.** The envelope pushes *last* (rbgjl02), so a crash between member-cp and vouch-push leaves new tags present in GAR but absent from the envelope. Tags are registry reality; the envelope can lag. So on every refresh (host-side, via augur machinery):

- **Present + enveloped** (tag in GAR ∧ member in envelope) → preserve the member entry **verbatim**, keeping its true per-member time. Never re-resolve upstream.
- **Present + orphaned** (tag in GAR ∧ absent from envelope — a crashed run's cp) → recover honestly from GAR: digest from its manifest, `rblv_acquired_at` = the GAR tag's **createTime**, verification `recorded`. No upstream re-touch.
- **Absent** (in widened selection, no GAR tag) → resolve from upstream, cp, time = NOW.

Only the **absent** group enters the cp/residency selection list; present+orphan are already resident. The envelope is re-authored to describe exactly the tags present after the run. Convergence holds because truth = tags and vouch is last: re-running after any crash lands an envelope matching reality, never re-resolves held bytes, never silently repoints a tag.

The host does all GAR reads (augur + per-orphan createTime via rbuh) and passes the already-present member set as a substitution; the select step's only upstream contact stays the absent leaves. That host-side present-set computation is the bulk of the new machinery.

## 4 · The podvm-only asymmetry (load-bearing — documented at the definition site)

Member-level times exist **iff a kind can accumulate members across multiple authoring events** — only podvm, only because it refreshes. I'll state this explicitly where the schema is defined (the `rblv_` legend), worded so a future uniformity sweep reads "this asymmetry tracks refresh-capability" and leaves it alone rather than "fixing" bole/wsl to match.

## 5 · Conversion surface (corrected from the directive)

- **Envelope authors → `rblv_` keys + schema bump:** rbgjl01, rbgjl03, rbgjl04, rbgjl07 (four).
- **Slot-format authors → `rbls_` keys:** same four `RESULT=` sites. The spine extract is **slot-agnostic** (base64-decodes the raw slot; doesn't parse keys) — so it's untouched.
- **Slot-format readers → `rbls_` jq paths:** the body extracts (rbldr, rbldv, and bole's extract).
- **Envelope reader → `rblv_` jq paths:** rbldl augur.
- **Fixtures:** insulated, mostly. The lifecycle fixtures assert on augur's *rendered text* (labels like "Trust grade:"), which the key rename doesn't touch — only the new podvm refresh-case assertions are fresh authoring. (I confirmed: the only envelope-field references in rbtdrc_crucible.rs are comments.)
- **rbgjl02 stays opaque** — wraps the envelope as a blob, never reads keys. Verify, don't convert.
- **Spec prose:** RBSLA, RBSLI, and any RBSL subdoc naming envelope fields.

## 6 · Settled
4KB: ~2.8 KB sprued at 8 members + per-member times — stands, envelope-in-slot stays. Fixture: the three tiers as you set them (recurring service = wsl-family fan-out + refresh, no test seam; fast = host-side `resolve_family` both-brands assertion; one-time gate = live 8-leaf native at verification, recorded in the wrap).

---

**Gate:** bless or amend the `rbls_` mint (and the two soft spots), and the v2 schema as drawn. On your nod I hand constants + body + fixture to sonnet against this schema and verify before the live run. Anything in the crash-resume rule or the podvm-only asymmetry you want to push on first?

local z_inscribe_ts=""
  local z_dockerfile_content=""
  # Cloud Build substitution values are limited to 4096 bytes. We use 4000 as a
  # conservative guard to account for encoding overhead and avoid edge-case failures.
  local -r z_dockerfile_max_bytes=4000

      z_inscribe_ts="${z_hallmark%%-r*}"

content.text:
**Palisade check resolved — Arch H stands, no contingency needed.** The project already characterized this limit: Cloud Build substitution values cap at **4096 bytes**, with **4000 as the conservative guard** (`rbfv_FoundryVerify.sh:438`). The 8-member worst case with per-member times, compact-serialized, is **~2.7 KB** — under 4000 with ~1.3 KB margin. So the host computes the preserved+orphan set via existing augur/rbuh machinery and passes it as one substitution; the select step never grows in-pool GAR-auth code. Recorded threshold: at ~340 bytes/member, Arch H holds through ~11 members — native full curation is 8, so we're clear, but a future widening past ~11 re-trips it and falls to in-pool recovery. That boundary goes in the body comment so it's a known edge, not a surprise 400.

Handing off to sonnet now against the blessed schema. I'll verify all of it — and run shellcheck + build + fast — before any live run.

TASK 4 — Refresh mode (Architecture H: host computes present-set, passes as ONE substitution)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
WHY refresh exists: podvm is recorded-grade against a hostile rotating upstream (quay deletes within days). Refresh = "expand the captured leaf set of an EXISTING Lode at the SAME version" without re-pulling/re-resolving already-held leaves (re-resolving could silently repoint a member tag to rebuilt bytes, or fail if upstream aged out). It is ADD-ONLY / PRESERVE-ORIGINALS.

INTERFACE:
- Fresh:   `rbw-lI <family> <version>`         → mints a new stamp `<kind-letter><YYMMDDHHMMSS>`, present-set empty, version from the arg.
- Refresh: `rbw-lI <family> --refresh <touchmark>` → reuses <touchmark> as the stamp; NO version argument (structurally cannot bump); the locked version is DERIVED from the target Lode's existing envelope (parse it from any member's rblv_origin, format "<family>:<version>").

SOURCE OF TRUTH = the GAR package's MEMBER TAGS, never the envelope (the envelope pushes last in rbgjl02, so a crash between member-cp and vouch-push leaves new tags present but un-enveloped).   • PRESENT + ORPHAN (tag in GAR ∧ absent from envelope) → recover honestly: digest from the tag's manifest, rblv_acquired_at = the GAR tag/version createTime, rblv_verification = "recorded". No upstream touch.
  • ABSENT (in widened selection, no GAR tag) → resolve from upstream in the cloud select step, cp, per-member time = NOW / this BUILD_ID.
Pass the preserved+orphan member objects to the select step as ONE NEW substitution (e.g. _RBGL_PODVM_PRESERVED), a COMPACT JSON array string; default "[]" for fresh capture (fresh is the empty-present-set case of the same path — keep one code path). Worst case 8 members ≈ 2.7KB < the 4000-byte conservative substitution guard (rbfv_FoundryVerify.sh:438 cites the 4096 hard limit) — add a comment noting Arch H holds through ~11 members and a future widening past that must fall to in-pool recovery.

SELECT STEP (rbgjl07-immure-select.py) merge logic:
  - Read the new _RBGL_PODVM_PRESERVED env var (JSON array; default []). Index it by member tag.
  - For each leaf in _RBGL_PODVM_SELECTION: if its member_tag is in the preserved index → splice the preserved member object straight into the envelope's rblv_members; do NOT fetch from quay; do NOT add it to /workspace/immure_selection.txt (the cp/residency contract).
  - Else (absent) → resolve from the quay index exactly as today, append to immure_selection.txt, and author its member with per-member rblv_acquired_at = the acquired_at timestamp and rblv_capture_build = os.environ["BUILD_ID"].
  - The final envelope's rblv_members = preserved ∪ orphan ∪ newly-resolved. The cp/residency selection list = newly-resolved (absent) only. Envelope-level rblv_acquired_at/rblv_capture_build = NOW / this build (the authoring event).
  - Keep the existing "die loud listing available (arch,disktype) on no-match" behavior for absent leaves.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
TASK 5 — Legend / asymmetry note (carry the architect's exact rationale)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
At the envelope's spec definition site (RBSLI-lode_immure.adoc, which owns the immure envelope; add a short pointer comment at the rbgjl07 author too), document TWO things in prose:
  (a) Per-member rblv_acquired_at/rblv_capture_build are PODVM-ONLY because only podvm refreshes — i.e. only podvm accumulates members across multiple authoring events. The asymmetry TRACKS refresh-capability; a future uniformity sweep must NOT "fix" bole/wsl to carry per-member times.
  (b) The dual-position rblv_acquired_at is SELF-DISCLOSING: in a refreshed envelope the member-level times sit EARLIER than the envelope-level time, so the artifact itself displays the authored-vs-captured distinction.
Update RBSLI's field list and RBSLA's augur-output description to the rblv_ names and the refresh mode. Preserve .adoc/MCM formatting.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
TASK 6 — Fixture (three tiers, NO new test seams)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  "rblv_members": [

}

TASK 4 — Refresh mode (Architecture H: host computes present-set, passes as ONE substitution)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
WHY refresh exists: podvm is recorded-grade against a hostile rotating upstream (quay deletes within days). Refresh = "expand the captured leaf set of an EXISTING Lode at the SAME version" without re-pulling/re-resolving already-held leaves (re-resolving could silently repoint a member tag to rebuilt bytes, or fail if upstream aged out). It is ADD-ONLY / PRESERVE-ORIGINALS.

INTERFACE:
- Fresh:   `rbw-lI <family> <version>`         → mints a new stamp `<kind-letter><YYMMDDHHMMSS>`, present-set empty, version from the arg.
- Refresh: `rbw-lI <family> --refresh <touchmark>` → reuses <touchmark> as the stamp; NO version argument (structurally cannot bump); the locked version is DERIVED from the target Lode's existing envelope (parse it from any member's rblv_origin, format "<family>:<version>").

SOURCE OF TRUTH = the GAR package's MEMBER TAGS, never the envelope (the envelope pushes last in rbgjl02, so a crash between member-cp and vouch-push leaves new tags present but un-enveloped). On refresh, compute the present-set HOST-SIDE (reuse the augur read path — list tags + read the existing :rbi_vouch envelope; for createTime use the GAR REST machinery in rbuh/rbgv that augur-adjacent code already uses). Classify each member-tag the widened selection would produce:

  • PRESENT + ORPHAN (tag in GAR ∧ absent from envelope) → recover honestly: digest from the tag's manifest, rblv_acquired_at = the GAR tag/version createTime, rblv_verification = "recorded". No upstream touch.
  • ABSENT (in widened selection, no GAR tag) → resolve from upstream in the cloud select step, cp, per-member time = NOW / this BUILD_ID.
Pass the preserved+orphan member objects to the select step as ONE NEW substitution (e.g. _RBGL_PODVM_PRESERVED), a COMPACT JSON array string; default "[]" for fresh capture (fresh is the empty-present-set case of the same path — keep one code path).   - For each leaf in _RBGL_PODVM_SELECTION: if its member_tag is in the preserved index → splice the preserved member object straight into the envelope's rblv_members; do NOT fetch from quay; do NOT add it to /workspace/immure_selection.txt (the cp/residency contract).
  - Else (absent) → resolve from the quay index exactly as today, append to immure_selection.txt, and author its member with per-member rblv_acquired_at = the acquired_at timestamp and rblv_capture_build = os.environ["BUILD_ID"].
  - The final envelope's rblv_members = preserved ∪ orphan ∪ newly-resolved. The cp/residency selection list = newly-resolved (absent) only. Envelope-level rblv_acquired_at/rblv_capture_build = NOW / this build (the authoring event).
  - Keep the existing "die loud listing available (arch,disktype) on no-match" behavior for absent leaves.

   readonly RBGC_LODE_PODVM_WSL_SELECTION="wsl:x86_64 wsl:aarch64"
-  readonly RBGC_LODE_PODVM_NATIVE_SELECTION="qemu:x86_64 wsl:x86_64"

+  # Sourced from memo-20260608 §5 (the machine-os index carries exactly these 8 disktype
+  # children at the 5.6 observation point; the 2 plain-container children are EXCLUDED
+  # by disktype-key selection). This pace (lode-podvm-platform-fanout) lands full
+  # curation; the "FOLLOWING pace" deferral is retired. Native full-curation is gated
+  # one-time (not a recurring service fixture) — see the podvm-lifecycle fixture comment.
+  readonly RBGC_LODE_PODVM_NATIVE_SELECTION="applehv:x86_64 applehv:aarch64 hyperv:x86_64 hyperv:aarch64 qemu:x86_64 qemu:aarch64 wsl:x86_64 wsl:aarch64"
 
   # rbi_df layout: flat namespace. No subdirs. Each filename names one
   # depot-scoped artifact; tag varies by artifact role.

content.text: Constant change is careful and well-cited — though I'll verify the native 8-leaf set against memo §5 myself (the `wsl:*` disktypes under the *native* family need checking). Now the design-bearing refresh draft:

content.content:
diff --git a/Tools/rbk/rbldv_Immure.sh b/Tools/rbk/rbldv_Immure.sh
index 7f6f31e4f..6ff6023d4 100644
--- a/Tools/rbk/rbldv_Immure.sh
+++ b/Tools/rbk/rbldv_Immure.sh
@@ -50,8 +50,8 @@ set -euo pipefail
 
 # Internal: resolve the family argument to its (kind-letter, quay-family, selection,
 # brand) tuple. The brand IS the operator-typed argument and the envelope kind field.
-# podvm-wsl is fixture-proven this pace; podvm-native is wired but its full curation +
-# refresh mode are the FOLLOWING pace (see rbgc_Constants podvm selection block).
+# Both families are wired; podvm-wsl is fixture-proven recurring (service tier), and
+# podvm-native carries full 8-leaf curation (see rbgc_Constants podvm selection block).
 # Args: family   Sets: z_kind, z_quay_family, z_selection (caller-scoped locals)
 zrbld_immure_resolve_family() {
   zrbld_sentinel
@@ -193,22 +193,35 @@ rbld_immure() {
   zrbld_sentinel
 
   buc_doc_brief "Wall in the selected podman-machine disk leaves of one quay family into a Lode (podvm kind, rbi_ld capture)"
-  buc_doc_param "family"  "Quay family — ${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_PODVM_WSL} or ${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_PODVM_NATIVE}"
-  buc_doc_param "version" "Podman version tag on the family index (e.g. 5.6)"
+  buc_doc_param "family"          "Quay family — ${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_PODVM_WSL} or ${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_PODVM_NATIVE}"
+  buc_doc_param "version|--refresh <touchmark>" \
+    "Fresh: version tag (e.g. 5.6); Refresh: literal '--refresh' followed by existing touchmark"
   buc_doc_shown || return 0
 
-  # Two declarative arguments (no FQIN — see RBSLI): the param1 channel routes the
-  # first to BUZ_FOLIO and forwards the rest, so family is the folio and version the
-  # first positional. The curated leaf-set is a per-family constant; the cloud step
-  # resolves leaf digests from the live index at capture time.
+  # BUZ_FOLIO carries the family argument (param1 channel). $1 is either the
+  # version (fresh) or the literal '--refresh' (refresh mode) with $2 as the
+  # existing touchmark. Refresh reuses the same stamp (no version bump possible);
+  # the locked version is derived from any member's rblv_origin in the existing envelope.
   local -r z_brand="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
-  local -r z_version="${1:-}"
-  test -n "${z_brand}"   || buc_die "family argument required (${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_PODVM_WSL} or ${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_PODVM_NATIVE})"
-  test -n "${z_version}" || buc_die "version argument required (e.g. 5.6)"
+  test -n "${z_brand}" || buc_die "family argument required (${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_PODVM_WSL} or ${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_PODVM_NATIVE})"
+
+  local z_mode="fresh"
+  local z_version=""
+  local z_stamp=""
+
+  if [ "${1:-}" = "--refresh" ]; then
+    z_mode="refresh"
+    local -r z_touchmark="${2:-}"
+    test -n "${z_touchmark}" || buc_die "touchmark required after --refresh (e.g. vw260610095327)"
+    z_stamp="${z_touchmark}"
+    buc_info "Immure REFRESH mode: reusing existing Lode ${z_touchmark}"
+  else
+    z_version="${1:-}"
+    test -n "${z_version}" || buc_die "version argument required (e.g. 5.6), or use --refresh <touchmark> for refresh mode"
+  fi
 
   local z_kind="" z_quay_family="" z_selection=""
   zrbld_immure_resolve_family "${z_brand}"
-  buc_info "Immure family: ${z_brand} (${z_quay_family}:${z_version}), leaves: ${z_selection}"
 
   buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
   source "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source Director RBRA"
@@ -218,18 +231,96 @@ rbld_immure() {
   z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
     || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"
 
-  # Mint the Lode stamp on the host: <kind-letter><YYMMDDHHMMSS>. The host owns the
-  # stamp so the touchmark is known before the build for the capture-file. podvm
-  # kind-letters are two characters (vw/vn); the augur touchmark regex accepts the
-  # multi-letter prefix.
-  local -r z_stamp="${z_kind}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:2:6}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:9:6}"
+  # Fresh mode: mint a new stamp and compute the preserved substitution (empty — no
+  # prior envelope exists). Refresh mode: reuse the existing stamp and compute the
+  # present-set from the existing GAR envelope + member tags.
+  local z_preserved_members="[]"
+  if [ "${z_mode}" = "fresh" ]; then
+    # Mint the Lode stamp on the host: <kind-letter><YYMMDDHHMMSS>. The host owns
+    # the stamp so the touchmark is known before the build for the capture-file.
+    # podvm kind-letters are two characters (vw/vn).
+    z_stamp="${z_kind}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:2:6}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:9:6}"
+    buc_info "Immure FRESH: ${z_brand} (${z_quay_family}:${z_version}), stamp: ${z_stamp}"
+  else
+    # Refresh: derive locked version from the existing envelope and compute the
+    # present-set (enveloped + orphan members) to pass as _RBGL_PODVM_PRESERVED.
+    buc_step "Computing present-set for refresh (reading existing :rbi_vouch envelope)"
+    local -r z_vouch_dir="${ZRBLD_IMMURE_PREFIX}refresh_vouch"
+    rm -rf "${z_vouch_dir}" || buc_die "Failed to clear refresh vouch scratch dir"
+    local -r z_pkg="${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
+    zrbfc_gar_extract_artifact "${z_token}" "${z_pkg}" "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH}" "${z_vouch_dir}" \
+      || buc_die "No :${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH} at ${z_pkg} — touchmark not present or not yet vouched"
+    local -r z_vouch_json="${z_vouch_dir}/vouch.json"
+    test -f "${z_vouch_json}" || buc_die "vouch.json missing in :${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH} artifact for ${z_stamp}"
+
+    # Derive the locked version from any member's rblv_origin (format "<family>:<version>").
+    z_version=$(jq -r '(.rblv_members // [])[0].rblv_origin | split(":")[1] // empty' "${z_vouch_json}") \
+      || buc_die "Failed to derive version from existing envelope for ${z_stamp}"
+    test -n "${z_version}" || buc_die "Existing envelope carries no rblv_origin in rblv_members — cannot derive version"
+    buc_info "Refresh locked to version: ${z_version} (from existing envelope)"
+    buc_info "Refresh stamp (reused): ${z_stamp}"
+
+    # Compute the present-set: members already in the existing envelope (preserve verbatim)
+    # plus any orphan tags in GAR (tag present but absent from envelope → recover).
+    # SOURCE OF TRUTH = the GAR package's member tags (never the envelope alone).
+    # Architecture H: the full present-set is passed as ONE substitution to the select step.
+    # Ceiling note: worst case 8 members ≈ 2.7 KB < 4000-byte substitution limit (CBh_103).
+    # A future widening past ~11 members must fall to in-pool recovery instead.
+    local -r z_tags_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg//\//%2F}/tags?pageSize=1000"
+    local -r z_tags_infix="rbld_immure_refresh_tags"
+    rbuh_json "GET" "${z_tags_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_tags_infix}"
+    rbuh_require_ok "List tags for ${z_stamp}" "${z_tags_infix}"
+    local -r z_resp_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_tags_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"
+
+    # Build preserved-member JSON array: enveloped members get verbatim rblv_members[] entry;
+    # orphan tags (in GAR, absent from envelope) get a recovered entry with rblv_verification
+    # "recorded" and rblv_acquired_at from the GAR tag createTime.
+    local -r z_preserved_file="${ZRBLD_IMMURE_PREFIX}preserved.json"
+    jq -r \
+      --arg sprue "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_SPRUE}" \
+      --arg vouch "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH}" \
+      --arg family "${z_quay_family}" \
+      --arg version "${z_version}" \
+      --argjson existing_members "$(jq '.rblv_members // []' "${z_vouch_json}")" \
+      '
+        # All non-vouch member tags present in GAR
+        ( [.tags[]?.name | sub(".*/tags/"; "") | select(. != $vouch and startswith($sprue))] ) as $gar_tags |
+        # Index existing envelope members by rblv_name
+        ( $existing_members | map({(.rblv_name): .}) | add // {} ) as $env_idx |
+        # For each GAR tag: enveloped -> preserve verbatim; orphan -> recover
+        [ $gar_tags[] |
+          if $env_idx[.] then
+            $env_idx[.]
+          else
+            {
+              "rblv_name": .,
+              "rblv_origin": ($family + ":" + $version),
+              "rblv_digest": "(recovered-orphan)",
+              "rblv_verification": "recorded",
+              "rblv_tags": [.],
+              "rblv_acquired_at": "unknown",
+              "rblv_capture_build": "unknown"
+            }
+          end
+        ]
+      ' "${z_resp_file}" > "${z_preserved_file}" \
+      || buc_die "Failed to compute present-set for refresh of ${z_stamp}"
+
+    z_preserved_members=$(jq -c '.' "${z_preserved_file}") \
+      || buc_die "Failed to compact preserved-member JSON"
+    local z_pcount=""
+    z_pcount=$(jq 'length' "${z_preserved_file}") \
+      || buc_die "Failed to count preserved members"
+    buc_info "Present-set computed: ${z_pcount} existing members to preserve/recover"
+  fi
 
   buc_info "Lode: ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
 
-  zrbld_immure_submit "${z_token}" "${z_brand}" "${z_quay_family}" "${z_version}" "${z_selection}" "${z_stamp}"
+  zrbld_immure_submit "${z_token}" "${z_brand}" "${z_quay_family}" "${z_version}" \
+    "${z_selection}" "${z_stamp}" "${z_preserved_members}"
   zrbld_immure_extract "${z_brand}"
 
-  buc_success "Immure complete: ${z_quay_family}:${z_version} -> ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
+  buc_success "Immure (${z_mode}) complete: ${z_quay_family}:${z_version} -> ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
 }
 
 # eof

content.text:
The overflow was my fault — that prompt packed six tasks and a full schema into one agent, and it drowned reading the big spec/fixture files. But the partial work is a genuine head start, and I've assessed it:

- **`rbgc_Constants.sh` (constant + schema bump):** solid. Schema → `rbld-vouch-2`, native curation → the full 8 `{applehv,hyperv,qemu,wsl}×{x86_64,aarch64}`. One thing I'll verify against memo §5 myself — `wsl:*` disktypes under the *native* family look surprising.
- **`rbldv_Immure.sh` (refresh body):** a good Arch-H draft — correct `--refresh` interface, version-derivation from `rblv_origin`, GAR-tags-as-truth. Two gaps from the overflow: orphan recovery is **stubbed dishonestly** (`"(recovered-orphan)"` digest, `"unknown"` times — my design wants the real digest from the GAR tag→version and `acquired_at` from createTime), and the wiring is unfinished (the `_RBGL_PODVM_PRESERVED` substitution is computed but `zrbld_immure_submit` and the select step don't consume it yet).

**Re-plan** (and a small, deliberate shift from Fable's "sonnet implements"): I take the two **design-bearing** files myself — `rbgjl07` (sprue + the merge logic) and finishing `rbldv` (submit wiring + honest orphan recovery) — where correctness is subtle and I hold the design. Then I delegate the genuinely **mechanical** remainder to tightly-scoped sonnet agents with short prompts: the sprue fan-out across the other three authors + body extracts + augur + the ACG line, and separately the fixture + spec prose. Smaller blast radius per agent, no overflow.

Nothing here is committed; I'll notch once it's whole and verified. Starting on the select step now.

input.old_string:

content.text: Now the loop, envelope, and result block — the core merge logic plus the rblv_/rbls_ sprue:

input.old_string:
for entry in selection.split():
        if ":" not in entry:
            die(f"malformed selection entry '{entry}' (want disktype:arch)")
        disktype, arch = entry.split(":", 1)
        member_tag = f"{sprue}{disktype}-{arch}"
        print(f"--- Selecting {disktype}/{arch} -> :{member_tag} ---")

        leaf_digest = None
        for m in descriptors:
            plat = m.get("platform") or {}
            ann = m.get("annotations") or {}
            if plat.get("architecture") == arch and ann.get("disktype", "") == disktype:
                leaf_digest = m.get("digest")
                break
        if not leaf_digest:
            # Die listing every available (architecture, disktype) pair, so a quay
            # rotation reads as a clear inventory diff, not a mystery.
            available = sorted({
                ((m.get("platform") or {}).get("architecture", ""),
                 (m.get("annotations") or {}).get("disktype", ""))
                for m in descriptors
            })
            die(f"no leaf for {disktype}/{arch} in {family}:{version}; "
                f"available (architecture, disktype): {available}")

        # Each disk leaf is a single-platform OCI artifact: empty config + exactly ONE
        # zstd blob. More than one layer means the descriptor matched a non-disk
        # manifest — die loud rather than residency-check one blob and ignore the rest.
        leaf = fetch_manifest(registry, repo, leaf_digest, token, ACCEPT_MANIFEST)
        layers = leaf.get("layers") or []
        if len(layers) != 1:
            die(f"leaf {disktype}/{arch} has {len(layers)} layers (expected 1 disk blob)")
        blob_digest = layers[0].get("digest")
        blob_size = layers[0].get("size")
        if not blob_digest or blob_size is None:
            die(f"leaf {disktype}/{arch} missing layer digest/size")
        print(f"  leaf manifest: {leaf_digest}")
        print(f"  disk blob:     {blob_digest} ({blob_size} bytes)")

        rows.append(f"{member_tag}|{leaf_digest}|{blob_digest}|{blob_size}")
        # Recorded grade: the attestation IS the captured leaf digest (the `digest`
        # field); origin keeps the declared family:version so the rotating-upstream
        # provenance survives in the envelope. verification "recorded" never implies
        # the bytes remain re-checkable against a vanished source.
        members.append({
            "name": member_tag,
            "origin": f"{family}:{version}",
            "digest": leaf_digest,
            "verification": "recorded",
            "tags": [member_tag],
        })

    if not rows:
        die("selection produced no leaves — nothing to immure")

    envelope = {
        "schema": require_env("_RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA"),
        "kind": brand,
        "lode": stamp,
        "acquired_at": acquired_at,
        "acquired_by": require_env("_RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY"),
        "capture_build": os.environ.get("BUILD_ID", ""),
        "trust_grade": require_env("_RBGL_TRUST_GRADE"),
        "signature": None,
        "members": members,
    }

    # Truncate-then-write (idempotent under GCB retry — CBi_103; no append mode).
    with open("/workspace/immure_selection.txt", "w") as f:
        f.write("\n".join(rows) + "\n")
    with open(f"/workspace/lode_{stamp}_vouch.json", "w") as f:
        json.dump(envelope, f)
    with open("/workspace/lode_stamps.txt", "w") as f:
        f.write(stamp + "\n")

    # Host-facing result (the capture-file carries the same envelope). One slot —
    # immure produces exactly one Lode (the cohort is one package). The spine
    # extracts buildStepOutputs from THIS step (index 0); pin the extract slot to 0.
    result = {"slot_1": {"stamp": stamp, "vouch": envelope}}
input.new_string:
for entry in selection.split():
        if ":" not in entry:
            die(f"malformed selection entry '{entry}' (want disktype:arch)")
        disktype, arch = entry.split(":", 1)
        member_tag = f"{sprue}{disktype}-{arch}"

        # Add-only / preserve-originals: a member the host reports as already captured
        # (enveloped verbatim, or orphan-recovered from GAR) is spliced in WITHOUT a
        # fresh upstream resolve and WITHOUT a capture row — its held bytes and its true
        # per-member time stand. Only genuinely-absent leaves touch the rotating
        # upstream. Fresh capture is the empty-preserved case (every leaf is absent).
        if member_tag in preserved_idx:
            print(f"--- Preserving {member_tag} (already captured; not re-resolved) ---")
            members.append(preserved_idx[member_tag])
            continue

        print(f"--- Selecting {disktype}/{arch} -> :{member_tag} ---")
        leaf_digest = None
        for m in descriptors:
            plat = m.get("platform") or {}
            ann = m.get("annotations") or {}
            if plat.get("architecture") == arch and ann.get("disktype", "") == disktype:
                leaf_digest = m.get("digest")
                break
        if not leaf_digest:
            # Die listing every available (architecture, disktype) pair, so a quay
            # rotation reads as a clear inventory diff, not a mystery.
            available = sorted({
                ((m.get("platform") or {}).get("architecture", ""),
                 (m.get("annotations") or {}).get("disktype", ""))
                for m in descriptors
            })
            die(f"no leaf for {disktype}/{arch} in {family}:{version}; "
                f"available (architecture, disktype): {available}")

        # Each disk leaf is a single-platform OCI artifact: empty config + exactly ONE
        # zstd blob. More than one layer means the descriptor matched a non-disk
        # manifest — die loud rather than residency-check one blob and ignore the rest.
        leaf = fetch_manifest(registry, repo, leaf_digest, token, ACCEPT_MANIFEST)
        layers = leaf.get("layers") or []
        if len(layers) != 1:
            die(f"leaf {disktype}/{arch} has {len(layers)} layers (expected 1 disk blob)")
        blob_digest = layers[0].get("digest")
        blob_size = layers[0].get("size")
        if not blob_digest or blob_size is None:
            die(f"leaf {disktype}/{arch} missing layer digest/size")
        print(f"  leaf manifest: {leaf_digest}")
        print(f"  disk blob:     {blob_digest} ({blob_size} bytes)")

        rows.append(f"{member_tag}|{leaf_digest}|{blob_digest}|{blob_size}")
        # Recorded grade: the attestation IS the captured leaf digest; origin keeps the
        # declared family:version so the rotating-upstream provenance survives. The
        # per-member rblv_acquired_at / rblv_capture_build are PODVM-ONLY — only podvm
        # refreshes, so only podvm accumulates members across multiple authoring events;
        # in a refreshed Lode the preserved members' times sit EARLIER than the
        # envelope-level rblv_acquired_at, so the artifact self-discloses the
        # authored-vs-captured split (see RBSLI). A uniformity sweep must not graft
        # these onto the single-capture kinds.
        members.append({
            "rblv_name": member_tag,
            "rblv_origin": f"{family}:{version}",
            "rblv_digest": leaf_digest,
            "rblv_verification": "recorded",
            "rblv_tags": [member_tag],
            "rblv_acquired_at": acquired_at,
            "rblv_capture_build": os.environ.get("BUILD_ID", ""),
        })

    if not members:
        die("selection produced no members — nothing to immure")

    envelope = {
        "rblv_schema": require_env("_RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA"),
        "rblv_kind": brand,
        "rblv_lode": stamp,
        "rblv_acquired_at": acquired_at,
        "rblv_acquired_by": require_env("_RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY"),
        "rblv_capture_build": os.environ.get("BUILD_ID", ""),
        "rblv_trust_grade": require_env("_RBGL_TRUST_GRADE"),
        "rblv_signature": None,
        "rblv_members": members,
    }

    # Truncate-then-write (idempotent under GCB retry — CBi_103; no append mode).
    # immure_selection.txt carries ONLY the absent leaves (the cp/residency contract);
    # on an all-preserved refresh it is legitimately empty and rbgjl08/09 no-op.
    with open("/workspace/immure_selection.txt", "w") as f:
        f.write("\n".join(rows) + ("\n" if rows else ""))
    with open(f"/workspace/lode_{stamp}_vouch.json", "w") as f:
        json.dump(envelope, f)
    with open("/workspace/lode_stamps.txt", "w") as f:
        f.write(stamp + "\n")

    # Host-facing result (the capture-file carries the same envelope). One slot —
    # immure produces exactly one Lode (the cohort is one package). The spine
    # extracts buildStepOutputs from THIS step (index 0); pin the extract slot to 0.
    result = {"rbls_slot_1": {"rbls_stamp": stamp, "rbls_vouch": envelope}}

content.content:
76	# Internal: compose the immure capture recipe (anon-quay index select + gcrane
77	# cp-by-digest + blob-residency guard + vouch-push) and its substitutions blob, then
78	# ride the capture spine to submit and poll. The spine owns the capture-domain build
79	# knobs (mason SA, TETHER pool, regime timeout); this body chooses only the recipe,
80	# the substitutions, and the inscribe-grade poll ceiling (the multi-GB leaf copies
81	# want the larger ceiling). Four steps across three builders: index-select rides the
82	# gcloud builder (python3 — parses the upstream OCI index, which the no-jq bash GCB
83	# discipline does not cover; rbgjl06 precedent); gcrane cp and the vouch-push ride the
84	# floating gcrane builder (busybox); the residency HEAD rides the Debian docker builder
85	# (curl, allowlisted). The recipe-row ORDER is part of the contract: vouch (rbgjl02)
86	# runs strictly after residency (rbgjl09) — the vouch artifact never precedes the
87	# anti-hollow-mirror guard. podvm is vessel-less (no reliquary slot), so its gcrane

file.content:
# Internal: compose the immure capture recipe (anon-quay index select + gcrane
# cp-by-digest + blob-residency guard + vouch-push) and its substitutions blob, then
# ride the capture spine to submit and poll. The spine owns the capture-domain build
# knobs (mason SA, TETHER pool, regime timeout); this body chooses only the recipe,
# the substitutions, and the inscribe-grade poll ceiling (the multi-GB leaf copies
# want the larger ceiling). Four steps across three builders: index-select rides the
# gcloud builder (python3 — parses the upstream OCI index, which the no-jq bash GCB
# discipline does not cover; rbgjl06 precedent); gcrane cp and the vouch-push ride the
# floating gcrane builder (busybox); the residency HEAD rides the Debian docker builder
# (curl, allowlisted). The recipe-row ORDER is part of the contract: vouch (rbgjl02)
# runs strictly after residency (rbgjl09) — the vouch artifact never precedes the
# anti-hollow-mirror guard. podvm is vessel-less (no reliquary slot), so its gcrane
# rides the floating bootstrap builder, same tier as conclave/wsl — pinning defers to
# the bootstrap-builder digest-pin itch (RBS0 rbsk_pinning_boundary).
# Args: token brand quay_family version selection stamp
zrbld_immure_submit() {
  zrbld_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:?Token required}"
  local -r z_brand="${2:?Brand required}"
  local -r z_quay_family="${3:?Family required}"
  local -r z_version="${4:?Version required}"
  local -r z_selection="${5:?Selection required}"
  local -r z_stamp="${6:?Stamp required}"

  buc_step "Constructing immure capture recipe"
  local -r z_gar_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
  local -r z_gar_path="${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"

  # Recipe rows: script_path|builder_image|id|entrypoint, pre-resolved for the spine.
  # Select on the gcloud builder (python3 — index parse); residency on the Debian docker
  # builder (curl HEAD); cp + vouch on the floating gcrane builder (busybox). The gcrane
  # builder reads public quay anonymously and pushes GAR ambiently (google.Keychain ->
  # Mason SA).
  local -r z_recipe=(
    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl07-immure-select.py|${ZRBLD_GCLOUD_BUILDER}|immure-select|python3"
    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl08-immure-capture.sh|${ZRBLD_GCRANE_BUILDER}|immure-capture|busybox"
    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl09-immure-residency.sh|${ZRBLD_GOOGLE_DOCKER_BUILDER}|immure-residency|bash"
    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl02-assemble-push-vouch.sh|${ZRBLD_GCRANE_BUILDER}|assemble-push-vouch|busybox"
  )

  buc_log_args "Composing immure substitutions blob"
  local -r z_subs_file="${ZRBLD_IMMURE_PREFIX}subs.json"
  jq -n \
    --arg zjq_gar_host     "${z_gar_host}" \
    --arg zjq_gar_path     "${z_gar_path}" \
    --arg zjq_lodes_root   "${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}" \
    --arg zjq_tag_sprue    "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_SPRUE}" \
    --arg zjq_tag_vouch    "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH}" \
    --arg zjq_trust_grade  "${RBGC_LODE_TRUST_RECORDED}" \
    --arg zjq_vouch_schema "${RBGC_LODE_VOUCH_SCHEMA}" \
    --arg zjq_acquired_by  "${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" \
    --arg zjq_stamp        "${z_stamp}" \
    --arg zjq_brand        "${z_brand}" \
    --arg zjq_family       "${z_quay_family}" \
    --arg zjq_version      "${z_version}" \
    --arg zjq_selection    "${z_selection}" \
    '{
      _RBGL_GAR_HOST:        $zjq_gar_host,
      _RBGL_GAR_PATH:        $zjq_gar_path,
      _RBGL_LODES_ROOT:      $zjq_lodes_root,
      _RBGL_TAG_SPRUE:       $zjq_tag_sprue,
      _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH:       $zjq_tag_vouch,
      _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE:     $zjq_trust_grade,
      _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA:    $zjq_vouch_schema,
      _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY:     $zjq_acquired_by,
      _RBGL_LODE_STAMP:      $zjq_stamp,
      _RBGL_PODVM_BRAND:     $zjq_brand,
      _RBGL_PODVM_FAMILY:    $zjq_family,
      _RBGL_PODVM_VERSION:   $zjq_version,
      _RBGL_PODVM_SELECTION: $zjq_selection
    }' > "${z_subs_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to compose immure substitutions blob"

  zrbld_spine_dispatch \
    "${z_token}" "${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" "Immure" "${ZRBFC_BUILD_POLL_CEILING_INSCRIBE}" \
    "${z_subs_file}" "${ZRBLD_IMMURE_PREFIX}" \
    "${z_recipe[@]}"
}

# Internal: extract the captured touchmark from the completed immure build and emit
# the two bare single-form chaining facts (touchmark value + kind-brand enum). The
# select step (step 0) authors the base64 JSON carrying the host-minted stamp in
# slot_1; the capture/residency/vouch steps write no output. Immure captures exactly
# one Lode (the cohort is one package), so exactly one slot is populated. The
# provenance envelope is NOT read host-side: it lives only in GAR (rbgjl02 pushed it
# under :rbi_vouch), so the host hands forward only the touchmark a consumer needs.
# Args: brand
zrbld_immure_extract() {
  zrbld_sentinel
  local -r z_brand="${1:?Brand required}"

  buc_step "Extracting capture results from build step outputs"

  local -r z_output_file="${ZRBLD_IMMURE_PREFIX}output.json"
  zrbld_spine_extract 0 "${z_output_file}"

  buc_log_args "Immure output:"
  buc_log_pipe < "${z_output_file}"

  local -r z_stamp_file="${ZRBLD_IMMURE_PREFIX}stamp.txt"
  jq -r '.slot_1.stamp // empty' "${z_output_file}" > "${z_stamp_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to read podvm stamp from immure output"
  local -r z_stamp=$(<"${z_stamp_file}")
  test -n "${z_stamp}" || buc_die "Immure output carried no stamp in slot_1"

  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_LODE_TOUCHMARK}" "${z_stamp}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to write touchmark fact for ${z_stamp}"
  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_LODE_BRAND}" "${z_brand}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to write kind-brand fact for ${z_stamp}"

local z_token=""
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"

  # Fresh mode: mint a new stamp and compute the preserved substitution (empty — no
  # prior envelope exists). Refresh mode: reuse the existing stamp and compute the
  # present-set from the existing GAR envelope + member tags.
  local z_preserved_members="[]"
  if [ "${z_mode}" = "fresh" ]; then
    # Mint the Lode stamp on the host: <kind-letter><YYMMDDHHMMSS>. The host owns
    # the stamp so the touchmark is known before the build for the capture-file.
    # podvm kind-letters are two characters (vw/vn).
    z_stamp="${z_kind}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:2:6}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:9:6}"
    buc_info "Immure FRESH: ${z_brand} (${z_quay_family}:${z_version}), stamp: ${z_stamp}"
  else
    # Refresh: derive locked version from the existing envelope and compute the
    # present-set (enveloped + orphan members) to pass as _RBGL_PODVM_PRESERVED.
    buc_step "Computing present-set for refresh (reading existing :rbi_vouch envelope)"
    local -r z_vouch_dir="${ZRBLD_IMMURE_PREFIX}refresh_vouch"
    rm -rf "${z_vouch_dir}" || buc_die "Failed to clear refresh vouch scratch dir"
    local -r z_pkg="${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
    zrbfc_gar_extract_artifact "${z_token}" "${z_pkg}" "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH}" "${z_vouch_dir}" \
      || buc_die "No :${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH} at ${z_pkg} — touchmark not present or not yet vouched"
    local -r z_vouch_json="${z_vouch_dir}/vouch.json"
    test -f "${z_vouch_json}" || buc_die "vouch.json missing in :${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH} artifact for ${z_stamp}"

    # Derive the locked version from any member's rblv_origin (format "<family>:<version>").
    z_version=$(jq -r '(.rblv_members // [])[0].rblv_origin | split(":")[1] // empty' "${z_vouch_json}") \
      || buc_die "Failed to derive version from existing envelope for ${z_stamp}"
    test -n "${z_version}" || buc_die "Existing envelope carries no rblv_origin in rblv_members — cannot derive version"
    buc_info "Refresh locked to version: ${z_version} (from existing envelope)"
    buc_info "Refresh stamp (reused): ${z_stamp}"

    # Compute the present-set: members already in the existing envelope (preserve verbatim)
    # plus any orphan tags in GAR (tag present but absent from envelope → recover).
    # SOURCE OF TRUTH = the GAR package's member tags (never the envelope alone).
    # Architecture H: the full present-set is passed as ONE substitution to the select step.
    # Ceiling note: worst case 8 members ≈ 2.7 KB < 4000-byte substitution limit (CBh_103).
    # A future widening past ~11 members must fall to in-pool recovery instead.
    local -r z_tags_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg//\//%2F}/tags?pageSize=1000"
    local -r z_tags_infix="rbld_immure_refresh_tags"
    rbuh_json "GET" "${z_tags_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_tags_infix}"
    rbuh_require_ok "List tags for ${z_stamp}" "${z_tags_infix}"
    local -r z_resp_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_tags_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"

    # Build preserved-member JSON array: enveloped members get verbatim rblv_members[] entry;
    # orphan tags (in GAR, absent from envelope) get a recovered entry with rblv_verification
    # "recorded" and rblv_acquired_at from the GAR tag createTime.
    local -r z_preserved_file="${ZRBLD_IMMURE_PREFIX}preserved.json"
    jq -r \
      --arg sprue "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_SPRUE}" \
      --arg vouch "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH}" \
      --arg family "${z_quay_family}" \
      --arg version "${z_version}" \
      --argjson existing_members "$(jq '.rblv_members // []' "${z_vouch_json}")" \
      '
        # All non-vouch member tags present in GAR
        ( [.tags[]?.name | sub(".*/tags/"; "") | select(. != $vouch and startswith($sprue))] ) as $gar_tags |
        # Index existing envelope members by rblv_name
        ( $existing_members | map({(.rblv_name): .}) | add // {} ) as $env_idx |
        # For each GAR tag: enveloped -> preserve verbatim; orphan -> recover
        [ $gar_tags[] |
          if $env_idx[.] then
            $env_idx[.]
          else
            {
              "rblv_name": .,
              "rblv_origin": ($family + ":" + $version),
              "rblv_digest": "(recovered-orphan)",
              "rblv_verification": "recorded",
              "rblv_tags": [.],
              "rblv_acquired_at": "unknown",
              "rblv_capture_build": "unknown"
            }
          end
        ]
      ' "${z_resp_file}" > "${z_preserved_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to compute present-set for refresh of ${z_stamp}"

    z_preserved_members=$(jq -c '.' "${z_preserved_file}") \
      || buc_die "Failed to compact preserved-member JSON"
    local z_pcount=""
    z_pcount=$(jq 'length' "${z_preserved_file}") \
      || buc_die "Failed to count preserved members"
    buc_info "Present-set computed: ${z_pcount} existing members to preserve/recover"
  fi

  buc_info "Lode: ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"

  zrbld_immure_submit "${z_token}" "${z_brand}" "${z_quay_family}" "${z_version}" \
    "${z_selection}" "${z_stamp}" "${z_preserved_members}"
  zrbld_immure_extract "${z_brand}"

  buc_success "Immure (${z_mode}) complete: ${z_quay_family}:${z_version} -> ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
}

content.text: The agent's orphan recovery writes fake values (`"(recovered-orphan)"`, `"unknown"`) — for recorded grade, where the attestation *is* "this digest, observed at this time," that's a hollow member. Let me replace it with honest recovery: real digest from the tag→version reference, `acquired_at` from the version's createTime (one added versions.list call).

input.old_string:
# Build preserved-member JSON array: enveloped members get verbatim rblv_members[] entry;
    # orphan tags (in GAR, absent from envelope) get a recovered entry with rblv_verification
    # "recorded" and rblv_acquired_at from the GAR tag createTime.
    local -r z_preserved_file="${ZRBLD_IMMURE_PREFIX}preserved.json"
    jq -r \
      --arg sprue "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_SPRUE}" \
      --arg vouch "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH}" \
      --arg family "${z_quay_family}" \
      --arg version "${z_version}" \
      --argjson existing_members "$(jq '.rblv_members // []' "${z_vouch_json}")" \
      '
        # All non-vouch member tags present in GAR
        ( [.tags[]?.name | sub(".*/tags/"; "") | select(. != $vouch and startswith($sprue))] ) as $gar_tags |
        # Index existing envelope members by rblv_name
        ( $existing_members | map({(.rblv_name): .}) | add // {} ) as $env_idx |
        # For each GAR tag: enveloped -> preserve verbatim; orphan -> recover
        [ $gar_tags[] |
          if $env_idx[.] then
            $env_idx[.]
          else
            {
              "rblv_name": .,
              "rblv_origin": ($family + ":" + $version),
              "rblv_digest": "(recovered-orphan)",
              "rblv_verification": "recorded",
              "rblv_tags": [.],
              "rblv_acquired_at": "unknown",
              "rblv_capture_build": "unknown"
            }
          end
        ]
      ' "${z_resp_file}" > "${z_preserved_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to compute present-set for refresh of ${z_stamp}"
input.new_string:
# Versions carry createTime (tags do not); fetch them too and join. An orphan is a
    # tag the crashed run cp'd but never enveloped — recover its digest from the tag's
    # own version reference and its rblv_acquired_at from that version's createTime.
    # Recorded grade attests a digest AT a time, so the time is load-bearing here, not
    # forensic decoration; rblv_capture_build stays null (the crashed build is gone).
    local -r z_versions_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg//\//%2F}/versions?pageSize=1000"
    local -r z_versions_infix="rbld_immure_refresh_versions"
    rbuh_json "GET" "${z_versions_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_versions_infix}"
    rbuh_require_ok "List versions for ${z_stamp}" "${z_versions_infix}"
    local -r z_versions_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_versions_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"

    # Build the preserved-member JSON array (the present-set). Enveloped members ride
    # verbatim (their true per-member times stand); orphan tags are recovered honestly.
    # SOURCE OF TRUTH = the GAR tags, never the envelope alone.
    local -r z_preserved_file="${ZRBLD_IMMURE_PREFIX}preserved.json"
    jq -n \
      --arg sprue "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_SPRUE}" \
      --arg vouch "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH}" \
      --arg family "${z_quay_family}" \
      --arg version "${z_version}" \
      --slurpfile tags_resp "${z_resp_file}" \
      --slurpfile vers_resp "${z_versions_file}" \
      --argjson existing_members "$(jq '.rblv_members // []' "${z_vouch_json}")" \
      '
        # sprued non-vouch tags, each carrying its digest (the tag -> version ref)
        ( [ $tags_resp[0].tags[]?
            | { tag: (.name | sub(".*/tags/"; "")),
                digest: ((.version // "") | sub(".*/versions/"; "")) }
            | select(.tag != $vouch and (.tag | startswith($sprue))) ] ) as $gar
        # digest -> createTime
        | ( [ $vers_resp[0].versions[]?
              | { (.name | sub(".*/versions/"; "")): .createTime } ] | add // {} ) as $vtime
        # rblv_name -> existing envelope member
        | ( $existing_members | map({(.rblv_name): .}) | add // {} ) as $env
        | [ $gar[]
            | if $env[.tag] then
                $env[.tag]
              else
                { rblv_name: .tag,
                  rblv_origin: ($family + ":" + $version),
                  rblv_digest: .digest,
                  rblv_verification: "recorded",
                  rblv_tags: [.tag],
                  rblv_acquired_at: ($vtime[.digest] // null),
                  rblv_capture_build: null }
              end ]
      ' > "${z_preserved_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to compute present-set for refresh of ${z_stamp}"

content.text: The two design-bearing files are complete. Now let me verify the native 8-leaf curation against memo §5 — I flagged that `wsl:*` disktypes under the *native* `machine-os` family looked surprising.

content.content:
=== memo §5 region ===
10:how Recipe Bottle should bring podman-VM machine-os images under control in GAR.
51:  specific `{disktype × arch}` leaves, and `crane cp <repo>@<leaf-digest>` each one.
93:| native `quay.io/podman/machine-os` | `5.6` | `sha256:6aa8bfeb41a8ce76b52277f5a609e5adeb097725ca4c3591c8ce2b4f4446dd47` |
94:| wsl `quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl` | `5.6` | `sha256:61bb25d36420d8eedf240cbb12deee90b6ff6e31e2d5aba224f537f8bcafdb05` |
114:  - 2 **plain** children `{arm64, amd64}`, no disktype → **real container OS images**
116:  - 8 **disktype** children `{aarch64, x86_64} × {applehv, hyperv, qemu, wsl}`,
117:    disktype carried in `annotations["disktype"]`, arch in the alt spelling

content.text:
Memo §5 confirms the agent's curation is correct — native `machine-os` 5.6 genuinely carries 8 disktype children `{applehv,hyperv,qemu,wsl}×{x86_64,aarch64}` (the `wsl`-disktype leaves are distinct digests from the wsl *family*; line 116). My surprise was unfounded. Constant verified.

The design-bearing core is done. Now the mechanical sprue sweep — a clean, bounded rename with an explicit map. I'll delegate that to a tightly-scoped sonnet agent (short prompt this time, no big-file reads) and run it in the background while I handle the specs and fixture.

content.content:
105	path — is known before the build
106	.. Example: `vw260610095327`
107	
108	//axhos_step
109	. *Submit Four-Step Capture Cloud Build*:
110	.. Compose `builds.create` JSON with:
111	... Step 1 — the Google-hosted gcloud builder, script `rbgjl07-immure-select.py` (python3: the index read + leaf select + envelope author)
112	... Step 2 — the floating gcrane builder, script `rbgjl08-immure-capture.sh` (`gcrane cp` each selected leaf by digest)
113	... Step 3 — the Google-hosted docker builder, script `rbgjl09-immure-residency.sh` (the blob-residency HEAD guard)
114	... Step 4 — the floating gcrane builder, script `rbgjl02-assemble-push-vouch.sh` (the vouch push)
115	... substitutions carrying GAR host/path, the
116	{gar_lodes_namespace}
117	root, `«TOUCHMARK»`, the member-tag sprue, the acquiring SA email, the recorded trust grade, the envelope schema, `«FAMILY»`, the version, and `«SELECTION»`
118	... serviceAccount:
119	{rbtr_mason}
120	... pool: tethered (the upstream index + leaf fetch requires public egress)
121	... `options.substitutionOption` ALLOW_LOOSE — the python step reads its substitutions as automapped env vars, not textual expansions, so the strict match would reject them
122	.. {rbbc_submit}
123	link:https://cloud.google.com/build/docs/api/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.builds/create[builds.create] with:
124	... project:
125	{rbtgi_depot_project}
126	... location:
127	{rbrd_gcp_region}
128	.. {rbbc_store}
129	`«BUILD_ID»` from response `.metadata.build.id`
130	.. {rbbc_show}
131	build dispatched with `«BUILD_ID»` and the Cloud Console URL
132	
133	//axhos_step
134	. *Select Step (in-pool — rbgjl07, python3)*:
135	.. Acquire an anonymous registry-v2 pull token for the public quay family (Www-Authenticate discovery)
136	.. Fetch the family index at the pinned version; fail loud if it is not a multi-arch index
137	.. For each curated `disktype:arch`, select the child descriptor matching `platform.architecture` + `annotations.disktype` — failing loud, listing every available pair, when a curated entry matches nothing (so a quay rotation reads as an inventory diff)
138	.. Fetch each selected leaf manifest; assert exactly one disk-blob layer; record its digest and declared size
139	.. Author the
140	{rbtga_lode_vouch}
141	envelope JSON (schema `rbld-vouch-1`, trust grade `recorded-at-acquisition`, `members[]` one element per leaf — the cardinality axis — each carrying `verification` `recorded` and the captured leaf digest) and stage it plus the selection list on the {rbtgr_workspace}
142	.. Write the result JSON (carrying the host-minted touchmark) to `buildStepOutputs`
143	
144	//axhos_step
145	. *Capture Step (in-pool — rbgjl08, gcrane)*:
146	.. For each selection row, `gcrane cp «FAMILY»@«LEAF_DIGEST»` into the one Lode package under the clean member tag `:rbi_<disktype>-<arch>` — by digest, get-or-error, daemonless
147	.. Confirm each copied tag resolves to the selected digest (a free manifest-level readback)
148	.. Each leaf is a single-platform manifest, so the package stays FLAT — no parent-index web — keeping banish single-call atomic by construction
149	
150	//axhos_step
151	. *Residency Step (in-pool — rbgjl09)*:
152	.. For each member, HEAD the GAR registry-v2 blob and assert its `Content-Length` equals the leaf manifest's declared layer size
153	.. The anti-hollow-mirror guard the recorded grade demands — a registry can hold a manifest whose large layer never fully landed, which the digest readback alone would not catch
154	
155	//axhos_step
156	. *Vouch Step (in-pool — rbgjl02)*:
157	.. For the staged envelope, build a `FROM scratch` image copying `vouch.json` and push it to the Lode package under the `:rbi_vouch` tag — a distinct manifest sharing the one package
158	.. The recipe order is load-bearing: the vouch push runs strictly after the residency guard, so the provenance artifact never precedes the integrity check
159	
160	//axhos_step
161	. *Wait for Build Completion*:
162	.. {rbbc_poll}
163	link:https://cloud.google.com/build/docs/api/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.builds/get[cloudbuild.builds.get] for `«BUILD_ID»`
164	.. {rbbc_require}
165	final status is `SUCCESS`
166	
167	//axhoot_typed_output
168	{rbf_fact_lode}
169	. *Write Capture-Facts*:
170	.. Read `buildStepOutputs[0]` (base64-decoded JSON) from the completed build to recover the host-minted `«TOUCHMARK»`
171	.. {rbbc_store}
172	the touchmark value and the podvm kind-brand enum (`podvm-wsl` / `podvm-native`, verbatim the family argument) as the two bare single-form facts of the
173	{rbf_fact_lode}
174	— carrying no provenance content; the envelope is the in-GAR `:rbi_vouch` tag, written cloud-side by the vouch step
175	
176	//axhoc_completion
177	Successful execution captures the selected podman-machine disk leaves into a single
178	{gar_service}
179	package at `{gar_lodes_namespace}/«TOUCHMARK»`, carrying each opaque disk blob under
180	its `:rbi_<disktype>-<arch>` member tag plus the `:rbi_vouch`
181	{rbtga_lode_vouch}
182	envelope, and hands the touchmark forward host-side as the
183	{rbf_fact_lode}.
184	+
185	The operation is capture-pure: no vessel or nameplate config is written.

189	+
190	Consumption is deferred this heat: immure stops at the registry.
191	A host's `podman machine init` from a captured seed, and the nameplate election that
192	pins which touchmark a node provisions, are a separate consumption-side layer that
