file.content:
path — is known before the build
.. Example: `vw260610095327`

//axhos_step
. *Submit Four-Step Capture Cloud Build*:
.. Compose `builds.create` JSON with:
... Step 1 — the Google-hosted gcloud builder, script `rbgjl07-immure-select.py` (python3: the index read + leaf select + envelope author)
... Step 2 — the floating gcrane builder, script `rbgjl08-immure-capture.sh` (`gcrane cp` each selected leaf by digest)
... Step 3 — the Google-hosted docker builder, script `rbgjl09-immure-residency.sh` (the blob-residency HEAD guard)
... Step 4 — the floating gcrane builder, script `rbgjl02-assemble-push-vouch.sh` (the vouch push)
... substitutions carrying GAR host/path, the
{gar_lodes_namespace}
root, `«TOUCHMARK»`, the member-tag sprue, the acquiring SA email, the recorded trust grade, the envelope schema, `«FAMILY»`, the version, and `«SELECTION»`
... serviceAccount:
{rbtr_mason}
... pool: tethered (the upstream index + leaf fetch requires public egress)
... `options.substitutionOption` ALLOW_LOOSE — the python step reads its substitutions as automapped env vars, not textual expansions, so the strict match would reject them
.. {rbbc_submit}
link:https://cloud.google.com/build/docs/api/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.builds/create[builds.create] with:
... project:
{rbtgi_depot_project}
... location:
{rbrd_gcp_region}
.. {rbbc_store}
`«BUILD_ID»` from response `.metadata.build.id`
.. {rbbc_show}
build dispatched with `«BUILD_ID»` and the Cloud Console URL

//axhos_step
. *Select Step (in-pool — rbgjl07, python3)*:
.. Acquire an anonymous registry-v2 pull token for the public quay family (Www-Authenticate discovery)
.. Fetch the family index at the pinned version; fail loud if it is not a multi-arch index
.. For each curated `disktype:arch`, select the child descriptor matching `platform.architecture` + `annotations.disktype` — failing loud, listing every available pair, when a curated entry matches nothing (so a quay rotation reads as an inventory diff)
.. Fetch each selected leaf manifest; assert exactly one disk-blob layer; record its digest and declared size
.. Author the
{rbtga_lode_vouch}
envelope JSON (schema `rbld-vouch-1`, trust grade `recorded-at-acquisition`, `members[]` one element per leaf — the cardinality axis — each carrying `verification` `recorded` and the captured leaf digest) and stage it plus the selection list on the {rbtgr_workspace}
.. Write the result JSON (carrying the host-minted touchmark) to `buildStepOutputs`

//axhos_step
. *Capture Step (in-pool — rbgjl08, gcrane)*:
.. For each selection row, `gcrane cp «FAMILY»@«LEAF_DIGEST»` into the one Lode package under the clean member tag `:rbi_<disktype>-<arch>` — by digest, get-or-error, daemonless
.. Confirm each copied tag resolves to the selected digest (a free manifest-level readback)
.. Each leaf is a single-platform manifest, so the package stays FLAT — no parent-index web — keeping banish single-call atomic by construction

//axhos_step
. *Residency Step (in-pool — rbgjl09)*:
.. For each member, HEAD the GAR registry-v2 blob and assert its `Content-Length` equals the leaf manifest's declared layer size
.. The anti-hollow-mirror guard the recorded grade demands — a registry can hold a manifest whose large layer never fully landed, which the digest readback alone would not catch

//axhos_step
. *Vouch Step (in-pool — rbgjl02)*:
.. For the staged envelope, build a `FROM scratch` image copying `vouch.json` and push it to the Lode package under the `:rbi_vouch` tag — a distinct manifest sharing the one package
.. The recipe order is load-bearing: the vouch push runs strictly after the residency guard, so the provenance artifact never precedes the integrity check

//axhos_step
. *Wait for Build Completion*:
.. {rbbc_poll}
link:https://cloud.google.com/build/docs/api/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.builds/get[cloudbuild.builds.get] for `«BUILD_ID»`
.. {rbbc_require}
final status is `SUCCESS`

//axhoot_typed_output
{rbf_fact_lode}
. *Write Capture-Facts*:
.. Read `buildStepOutputs[0]` (base64-decoded JSON) from the completed build to recover the host-minted `«TOUCHMARK»`
.. {rbbc_store}
the touchmark value and the podvm kind-brand enum (`podvm-wsl` / `podvm-native`, verbatim the family argument) as the two bare single-form facts of the
{rbf_fact_lode}
— carrying no provenance content; the envelope is the in-GAR `:rbi_vouch` tag, written cloud-side by the vouch step

//axhoc_completion
Successful execution captures the selected podman-machine disk leaves into a single
{gar_service}
package at `{gar_lodes_namespace}/«TOUCHMARK»`, carrying each opaque disk blob under
its `:rbi_<disktype>-<arch>` member tag plus the `:rbi_vouch`
{rbtga_lode_vouch}
envelope, and hands the touchmark forward host-side as the
{rbf_fact_lode}.
+
The operation is capture-pure: no vessel or nameplate config is written.
The captured cohort is a Director-dictated version closure — the substrate bytes are
exactly the digests captured from the pinned family/version, never "whatever the
operator's podman happened to fetch".
+
Consumption is deferred this heat: immure stops at the registry.
A host's `podman machine init` from a captured seed, and the nameplate election that
pins which touchmark a node provisions, are a separate consumption-side layer that
reads the capture-file — so election retries independently and survives, and a
one-to-many fan-out across nodes iterates by reading the file rather than by a verb
taking N node arguments.

//axhob_operation
{rbtgo_lode_immure}

NOTE: This operation captures the selected podman-machine disk leaves of one quay
family into a single
{rbtga_lode}
— one
{gar_service}
package under the
{gar_lodes_namespace}
namespace, identified by a host-minted
{rbtga_touchmark}.
It is the podvm kind: a defensive mirror of quay's podman-machine images, which the
upstream rotates within days and never publishes a durable checksum for.
Its payload shape is opaque-blob (like the wsl kind) and its cardinality is
multi-member (like the reliquary kind): each captured leaf is a single-platform OCI
artifact — an empty config plus one `application/zstd` disk blob — riding as one
member tag within the one package.

NOTE: One verb spans both quay families.
`immure` takes the family as its first argument — `podvm-wsl`
(`quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl`, consumed by Windows podman) or `podvm-native`
(`quay.io/podman/machine-os`, consumed by macOS/Linux podman) — and the podman
version as its second (e.g. `5.6`).
The two families diverge in version coverage and platform fan-out, so the kind-letter
(`vw` / `vn`), the upstream family reference, and the curated leaf set are all keyed
on the family argument; there is no kind-argument indirection beyond that one word.

NOTE: Intent is declarative — no FQIN, no pinned digest.
The curated `{disktype × arch}` leaf set is an RB constant per family; the
{rbtr_director}
's workstation assembles no coordinate beyond the family and version, and the cloud
step RESOLVES each leaf digest from the live index at capture time.
No upstream digest lives in project config — per the no-FQIN principle, the
declaration stays intent and the pipeline computes the coordinate.

NOTE: Acquisition runs cloud-side.
The
{rbtr_director}
submits a
{gcb_service}
job on the tethered (egress-permitted) pool, executed by the
{rbtr_mason}
service account via the metadata server.
The workstation never fetches the multi-gigabyte disk bytes — only the cloud step
copies them, registry-to-registry by digest.

NOTE: The trust grade is recorded-at-acquisition.
quay publishes no durable, re-checkable checksum for podvm and rotates the images
out within days, so RB attests only the leaf digest observed at the moment of
capture — trust-on-first-acquisition, the strongest honest claim the hostile
upstream permits.
The integrity guards are `gcrane cp`-by-digest (get-or-error, digest-faithful) and a
registry-v2 blob-residency check, NOT a re-checkable published checksum.

NOTE: Immure selects leaves by the index DESCRIPTOR, never the layer filename.
The upstream tag is a multi-arch OCI index mixing real container images and disk
artifacts; selection keys on each child descriptor's `platform.architecture` (the alt
spelling `x86_64` / `aarch64`) plus `annotations.disktype`.
The layer's embedded filename is unreliable for version and arch and is never read.

NOTE: Immure bootstraps from no reliquary.
podvm is vessel-less — like wsl — so it has no reliquary slot to resolve tools from;
its index parsing rides python3 on the Google-hosted gcloud builder, and its registry
copy rides gcrane on the floating gcrane builder.
Pinning these floating builders is the bootstrap-builder digest-pin itch, not this
operation.

NOTE: Immure is capture-pure.
It mints the Lode, emits the
{rbtga_lode_vouch}
provenance envelope, and reports the touchmark — but writes NO consumer config.
Consumption (a host's `podman machine init` from a captured seed, and the nameplate
election that pins which touchmark a node provisions) is a separate, deferred
consumption-side layer that reads the
{rbf_fact_lode}
this operation writes; it is not part of immure.

The
{rbtr_director}
captures a podvm family's disk leaves into a Lode:

//axhos_step
. *Authenticate as
{rbtr_director}*:
.. Execute
{rbtoe_director_authenticate}
.. {rbbc_store}
`«DIRECTOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls

//axhos_step
. *Resolve the family (host-side)*:
.. {rbbc_store}
`«KIND»` (`vw` / `vn`), `«FAMILY»` (the quay family reference), and `«SELECTION»`
(the curated `disktype:arch` rows) from the family argument
.. The family-to-tuple mapping and the curated leaf set are RB constants; the version
is the operator's (later, a nameplate's) declarative choice

//axhos_step
. *Mint Touchmark (host-side)*:
.. {rbbc_store}
`«TOUCHMARK»` as the kind-letter `«KIND»` + `YYMMDDHHMMSS` from current time
.. The host owns the stamp so the touchmark — and thus the
{rbf_fact_lode}

content.text: RBSLI is a typed operation spec. Let me make the field-sprue, schema-bump, refresh-mode, and the asymmetry/dual-position legend edits.

input.old_string:
The two families diverge in version coverage and platform fan-out, so the kind-letter
(`vw` / `vn`), the upstream family reference, and the curated leaf set are all keyed
on the family argument; there is no kind-argument indirection beyond that one word.
input.new_string:
The two families diverge in version coverage and platform fan-out, so the kind-letter
(`vw` / `vn`), the upstream family reference, and the curated leaf set are all keyed
on the family argument; there is no kind-argument indirection beyond that one word.

NOTE: Refresh widens an existing Lode at its locked version.
`immure «FAMILY» --refresh «TOUCHMARK»` reuses an existing Lode's touchmark and
captures only the leaves the widened curated set adds, leaving every already-held
member's bytes and provenance untouched (add-only).
It takes no version argument — the locked version is derived from the existing
envelope — so it can never become a version bump; advancing the podman version is a
fresh capture into a new touchmark.
Refresh exists because the recorded-grade upstream rotates within days: re-resolving a
held leaf could silently repoint its member tag to rebuilt bytes, so a held member is
never re-fetched.

//axhos_step

.. For the staged envelope, build a `FROM scratch` image copying `vouch.json` and push it to the Lode package under the `:rbi_vouch` tag — a distinct manifest sharing the one package
.. The recipe order is load-bearing: the vouch push runs strictly after the residency guard, so the provenance artifact never precedes the integrity check

authoring coincides with capture; the asymmetry tracks refresh-capability, and a
uniformity sweep must not graft them onto those kinds.

=== rbgjl08 (capture) empty-handling + read loop ===
#!/bin/bash
# RBGJL Step 08: Copy the selected podvm disk leaves into a Lode via gcrane (capture)
# Builder: gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug (floating bootstrap — podvm is
#          vessel-less like wsl, so its tool-pinning defers to the bootstrap-builder
#          digest-pin itch, RBS0 rbsk_pinning_boundary. gcrane reads the PUBLIC quay
#          source anonymously and auths the GAR push ambiently via google.Keychain
#          -> ADC -> the GCE metadata server as the Mason SA, no explicit login. The
#          :debug variant carries /busybox/sh. Auth canon: RBSCB)
# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT, _RBGL_LODE_STAMP,
#                _RBGL_PODVM_FAMILY
#
# Step 07 (python3 on the gcloud builder) anon-read the quay family index, selected the
# curated leaves by descriptor platform+disktype, and staged the selection list at
# /workspace/immure_selection.txt (one row: member_tag|leaf_digest|blob_digest|
# blob_size). This step gcrane-cp's each leaf BY DIGEST from the family repo into the
# ONE GAR package rbi_ld/<stamp> under its member tag, then confirms the copy is
# digest-faithful (cheap manifest readback; the blob Content-Length residency guard
# is rbgjl09). The split exists because parsing the structured upstream index belongs
# in python (CBG CBp_ rules), which the gcrane:debug busybox shell cannot host —
# registry copy is gcrane's job, index parsing is python's.
#
# gcrane cp <family>@<leaf-digest> is get-or-error (the loud failure the recorded
# trust grade wants) and preserves the manifest digest byte-for-byte (memo-20260608
# §4). Each selected leaf is a single-platform manifest, so the Lode package stays a
# FLAT package (no parent-index web) — banish stays single-call atomic by
# construction (RBSLB). Do not capture the family index itself; that would web the
# package.

set -euo pipefail
echo "=== Copy selected podvm disk leaves into a Lode (gcrane cp by digest) ==="

STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_STAMP}"
FAMILY="${_RBGL_PODVM_FAMILY}"
test -n "${STAMP}"  || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_LODE_STAMP missing"   >&2; exit 1; }
test -n "${FAMILY}" || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_PODVM_FAMILY missing" >&2; exit 1; }

# Guard the inter-step handoff (CBi_102): step 07 must have selected the leaves.
test -s /workspace/immure_selection.txt \
  || { echo "FATAL: /workspace/immure_selection.txt missing/empty — step 07 must run first" >&2; exit 1; }

PKG="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}"
echo "Lode package: ${PKG}"

while IFS='|' read -r MEMBER_TAG LEAF_DIGEST BLOB_DIGEST BLOB_SIZE; do
  test -n "${MEMBER_TAG}" || continue
  SRC="${FAMILY}@${LEAF_DIGEST}"
  DEST="${PKG}:${MEMBER_TAG}"
  echo "--- ${MEMBER_TAG}: ${SRC} -> ${DEST} ---"

  # Copy registry->registry by digest, daemonless (no docker pull/tag/push). The
  # leaf is a single-platform OCI artifact (empty config + one zstd blob); gcrane cp
  # copies the manifest and its blob and preserves the manifest digest.
  gcrane cp "${SRC}" "${DEST}" \
    || { echo "FATAL: gcrane cp failed for ${SRC} -> ${DEST}" >&2; exit 1; }

  # Cheap manifest-level integrity check (free on this builder): the copied tag must
  # resolve to the same digest we selected. The deeper blob Content-Length residency
  # guard is rbgjl09 (needs curl — a Debian step). CBb_101 applies.
  DEST_DIGEST=$(gcrane digest "${DEST}") \

content.text:
Two real issues surfaced — both correctness, both outranking the fixture:

1. **`rbgjl08` dies on an empty selection file** (`test -s ... || die`). An all-preserved refresh writes an empty selection (nothing new to capture) — so the convergence guarantee *and* the recurring wsl refresh proof would both crash here. The guard must distinguish "step 07 never ran" (file missing) from "nothing new to capture" (file present, empty).
2. **`rbgjl04` left `signing_key` bare** while every sibling is `rblv_`-sprued — a bare key inside a sprued envelope, defeating the census. It's wsl's per-member gpg fingerprint; it needs `rblv_signing_key`.

Let me check rbgjl09's guard too, then fix all three.

#!/bin/bash
# RBGJL Step 09: Blob-residency guard for the captured podvm leaves (anti-hollow-mirror)
# Builder: gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker (Google-hosted, Debian — carries curl; the
#          gcrane:debug capture builder has no curl, so the registry-v2 HEAD guard
#          lives here, after the cp). The Mason SA token comes from the metadata
#          server (rbgjs token-fetch).
# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT, _RBGL_LODE_STAMP
#
# Note: this script runs inside a Cloud Build container, not under BCG module
# discipline (CBG governs).
#
# Step 08 gcrane-cp'd each selected leaf into rbi_ld/<stamp> and confirmed the
# MANIFEST digest is faithful. This step confirms the BLOB BYTES actually reside: a
# GAR registry-v2 blob HEAD returns Content-Length; assert it equals the leaf
# manifest's declared layer size (staged by rbgjl07 in the selection list). This is
# the anti-hollow-mirror guard the recorded trust grade demands — a registry can
# hold a manifest whose large layer never fully landed; the digest check alone would
# not catch it (memo-20260608 §1, proven against 1.13 GB / 245 MB / 200 MB / 194 MB
# blobs). curl HEAD, no image tool, no blob download.

set -euo pipefail
echo "=== Blob-residency guard for captured podvm leaves ==="

STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_STAMP}"
test -n "${STAMP}" || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_LODE_STAMP missing" >&2; exit 1; }

# Guard the inter-step handoff (CBi_102): the selection list is the residency contract.
test -s /workspace/immure_selection.txt \
  || { echo "FATAL: /workspace/immure_selection.txt missing/empty — step 07 must run first" >&2; exit 1; }

# Mason SA OAuth token from the metadata server (provides TOKEN).
#@rbgjs_include token-fetch

GAR_IMAGE="${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}"
echo "GAR image path: ${GAR_IMAGE}"

while IFS='|' read -r MEMBER_TAG LEAF_DIGEST BLOB_DIGEST BLOB_SIZE; do
  test -n "${MEMBER_TAG}" || continue
  echo "--- Residency: ${MEMBER_TAG} (${BLOB_DIGEST}) expecting ${BLOB_SIZE} bytes ---"

  BLOB_URL="https://${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/v2/${GAR_IMAGE}/blobs/${BLOB_DIGEST}"
  HEAD_FILE="/workspace/immure_head_${MEMBER_TAG}.txt"
  curl -sfI -H "Authorization: Bearer ${TOKEN}" "${BLOB_URL}" -o "${HEAD_FILE}" \
    || { echo "FATAL: blob HEAD failed for ${MEMBER_TAG} at ${BLOB_URL}" >&2; exit 1; }

  # Content-Length, case-insensitive header, strip CR. awk's END keeps the last
  # match (a redirect chain would append; the registry-v2 endpoint answers HEAD
  # directly per the memo). grep+awk only — tail/tr are outside the GCB allowlist.
  ACTUAL_LEN=$(grep -i 'content-length:' "${HEAD_FILE}" | awk 'END{gsub(/\r/,"",$2); print $2}')
  test -n "${ACTUAL_LEN}" \
    || { echo "FATAL: no Content-Length in HEAD response for ${MEMBER_TAG}" >&2; exit 1; }

  if [ "${ACTUAL_LEN}" != "${BLOB_SIZE}" ]; then
    echo "FATAL: hollow mirror — ${MEMBER_TAG} blob Content-Length ${ACTUAL_LEN} != declared ${BLOB_SIZE}" >&2
    exit 1
  fi
  echo "${MEMBER_TAG}: ${ACTUAL_LEN} bytes resident (matches declared size)"
done < /workspace/immure_selection.txt

echo "=== Immure residency guard complete ==="

#!/bin/bash
# RBGJL Step 04: Fetch + GPG-verify a WSL rootfs and stage it for the wrap step
# Builder: gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker (Google-hosted, always pullable; Debian-based
#          — carries curl, and gnupg is apt-installed by the gpg-verify-sums snippet
#          if absent). The acquisition tool is curl over HTTPS, not a registry pull,
#          so this kind needs neither skopeo nor a reliquary bootstrap. This step does
#          NOT push: it fetches, verifies, authors the envelope, and stages rootfs.tar
#          for the gcrane-append wrap (rbgjl05) — the fetch/verify builder (curl+gpg,
#          Debian) and the wrap builder (gcrane:debug busybox) cannot be one image.
# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT, _RBGL_LODE_STAMP,
#                _RBGL_TAG_ROOTFS, _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE, _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA,
#                _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY, _RBGL_WSL_URL, _RBGL_WSL_KEY_FPR
#
# Fetch the vendor rootfs tarball (URL assembled host-side from the version args)
# over HTTPS, DISCOVER its checksum from Canonical's published, GPG-signed
# SHA256SUMS (verified against the pinned signing-key fingerprint — the
# verified-against-published gate), verify the rootfs bytes against it, then stage the
# OPAQUE tarball for the wrap step (rbgjl05), which gcrane-appends it as a single-layer
# OCI member (never extracted) into ONE GAR package rbi_ld/<stamp> under the clean
# member tag :rbi_rootfs. Author the batch provenance envelope (members[]
# length 1 — the cardinality axis) and stage it for step 02 (the :rbi_vouch
# artifact) and for the host capture-file via /builder/outputs/output.
# Single-platform (linux/amd64): the rootfs is an opaque blob, not a multi-arch
# manifest. No digest is pinned here — the published checksum is discovered and
# signature-verified at capture, per the no-FQIN premise (RBSLU).
#
# Package shape:  <host>/<path>/<LODES_ROOT>/<stamp>     (one package = one Lode)
# Member tag on that package (clean scheme — no digest/fingerprint layer):
#   :<TAG_ROOTFS>   e.g. rbi_rootfs   (the opaque rootfs blob)
# The :rbi_vouch tag is a separate manifest pushed by step 02.
#
# Trust grade verified-against-published: the member digest recorded in the
# envelope is the tarball's GPG-verified published SHA-256, tagged verification
# "gpg-sha256-published" with the signing-key fingerprint — NOT an OCI manifest
# digest, because what is attested is capture-fidelity of an opaque blob under the
# vendor's signed checksum.

set -euo pipefail
echo "=== Underpin WSL rootfs into a Lode ==="

STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_STAMP}"
test -n "${STAMP}" || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_LODE_STAMP missing" >&2; exit 1; }

URL="${_RBGL_WSL_URL}"
KEY_FPR="${_RBGL_WSL_KEY_FPR}"
test -n "${URL}"     || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_WSL_URL missing" >&2; exit 1; }
test -n "${KEY_FPR}" || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_WSL_KEY_FPR missing" >&2; exit 1; }

PKG="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}"
MEMBER_TAG="${_RBGL_TAG_ROOTFS}"
DEST="${PKG}:${MEMBER_TAG}"
echo "Lode package: ${PKG}"
echo "Source:       ${URL}"

# Acquisition moment, attested for the single-member cohort.
ACQUIRED_AT=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)

# --- Discover the published checksum under the pinned signing key ---
# The sums + signature live beside the tarball; the target filename is its
# basename. The snippet GPG-verifies the sums and provides EXPECTED_SHA.
SUMS_URL="${URL%/*}/SHA256SUMS"
SIG_URL="${SUMS_URL}.gpg"
TARGET_BASENAME="${URL##*/}"
#@rbgjs_include gpg-verify-sums

# --- Fetch the rootfs over HTTPS (cloud-side; the workstation never touches these
# bytes — only the URL was assembled host-side) ---
ROOTFS="/workspace/underpin_${STAMP}.rootfs.tar"
echo "--- Fetching rootfs tarball ---"
curl -fSL --retry 3 -o "${ROOTFS}" "${URL}" \
  || { echo "FATAL: Failed to fetch rootfs from ${URL}" >&2; exit 1; }

# --- Verify the rootfs bytes against the GPG-verified published checksum ---
# Tool availability on the docker builder is a Palisade: sha256sum (coreutils) is
# the expected path; shasum / openssl are honest fallbacks so a missing tool fails
# the compare loud rather than skipping verification.
echo "--- Verifying rootfs against published checksum ---"
if command -v sha256sum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  ACTUAL_SHA=$(sha256sum "${ROOTFS}" | cut -d' ' -f1)
elif command -v shasum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  ACTUAL_SHA=$(shasum -a 256 "${ROOTFS}" | cut -d' ' -f1)
elif command -v openssl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  ACTUAL_SHA=$(openssl dgst -sha256 "${ROOTFS}" | awk '{print $NF}')
else
  echo "FATAL: no sha256 tool (sha256sum/shasum/openssl) on builder — cannot verify" >&2
  exit 1
fi
test -n "${ACTUAL_SHA}" || { echo "FATAL: empty digest computed for rootfs" >&2; exit 1; }

if [ "${ACTUAL_SHA}" != "${EXPECTED_SHA}" ]; then
  echo "FATAL: rootfs checksum mismatch — refusing to capture unverified bytes" >&2
  echo "  published (GPG-verified) sha256: ${EXPECTED_SHA}" >&2
  echo "  fetched   sha256:                ${ACTUAL_SHA}" >&2
  exit 1
fi
echo "Verified: sha256:${ACTUAL_SHA} (matches GPG-signed published checksum)"

# --- Stage the verified OPAQUE tarball for the wrap step (rbgjl05) ---
# The rootfs is staged byte-for-byte into a context dir holding ONLY rootfs.tar;
# rbgjl05 gcrane-appends that dir as a FROM-scratch single-layer member (rootfs.tar at
# image root, never extracted — gcrane append wraps the blob, it does not unpack it).
# The wrap is a separate step because gcrane:debug busybox has no curl/gpg: this Debian
# builder fetches and verifies, the gcrane builder wraps and pushes.
echo "--- Staging verified rootfs for the wrap step -> ${DEST} ---"
CTX="/workspace/underpin_ctx_${STAMP}"
mkdir -p "${CTX}"
cp "${ROOTFS}" "${CTX}/rootfs.tar"

echo "Rootfs verified and staged: ${DEST} (sha256:${ACTUAL_SHA})"

# --- Author the batch provenance envelope (identical content lands in :rbi_vouch
# and the host capture-file). rblv_members[] length 1 — the wsl singleton. No jq
# dependency — values are controlled (member tag, URL, hex digest, key fingerprint,
# SA email, build id, ISO timestamp); none can carry a literal quote. ---
ENVELOPE='{'
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_schema\":\"${_RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA}\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_kind\":\"wsl\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_lode\":\"${STAMP}\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_acquired_at\":\"${ACQUIRED_AT}\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_acquired_by\":\"${_RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY}\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_capture_build\":\"${BUILD_ID:-}\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_trust_grade\":\"${_RBGL_TRUST_GRADE}\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_signature\":null,"
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_members\":[{"
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_name\":\"${MEMBER_TAG}\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_origin\":\"${URL}\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_digest\":\"sha256:${ACTUAL_SHA}\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_verification\":\"gpg-sha256-published\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"signing_key\":\"${KEY_FPR}\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_tags\":[\"${MEMBER_TAG}\"]"
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}}]}"

# Stage the envelope for step 02 (pushes it as the :rbi_vouch artifact). The
# stamps file is the step-02 contract; underpin produces exactly one Lode.
printf '%s' "${ENVELOPE}" > "/workspace/lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json"
: > /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
echo "${STAMP}" >> /workspace/lode_stamps.txt

# Host-facing result (the capture-file carries the same envelope). One slot —
# underpin produces exactly one Lode.
RESULT="{\"rbls_slot_1\":{\"rbls_stamp\":\"${STAMP}\",\"rbls_vouch\":${ENVELOPE}}}"

echo "=== Writing capture results ==="
echo "${RESULT}"

# Write to buildStepOutputs channel (host extracts the touchmark -> capture-file).
mkdir -p /builder/outputs
printf '%s' "${RESULT}" > /builder/outputs/output

echo "=== Underpin capture step complete ==="

content.content:
2210	The provenance envelope carried by every
2211	{rbtga_lode}
2212	— one per Lode, batch-level — recording *capture-fidelity* rather than build-integrity.
2213	The fetched-side parallel to the made-side
2214	{rbtga_ark_vouch},
2215	and a different claim: "we faithfully captured upstream X at digest D," not "we built this securely."
2216	+
2217	It has one canonical home: the in-GAR `:rbi_vouch` tag (the Lode self-describing in the registry, decoded by {rbtgo_lode_augur}). The host-side {rbf_fact_lode} carries only the bare touchmark, not the envelope — so reading host-side provenance costs a GAR call, the accepted price of a single canonical home.
2218	+
2219	Fields (schema `rbld-vouch-1`, near-term unsigned — `signature: null` — but schema-versioned):
2220	`schema`, `kind`, `lode` (the touchmark), `acquired_at` (ISO-8601 UTC), `acquired_by` (the capturing Cloud Build service account), `capture_build` (the capture's own build id), `trust_grade`, and `members[]`.
2221	Each `members[]` entry carries `name`, `origin`, `digest`, `verification`, and its assigned `tags` — so the captured identity and any Director-assigned names are themselves attested.
2222	`members[]` is the cardinality axis: length 1 for singletons (bole), N for cohorts.
2223	+
2224	Two honest trust grades, declared per Lode (Palisade honesty — never over-claim):
2225	+
2226	* `verified-against-published` — bytes re-checkable against a persistent upstream (OCI content-address, or an out-of-band published checksum). The bole kind carries this grade.
2227	* `recorded-at-acquisition` — upstream offers no durable re-checkable reference (e.g. quay rotates podvm images out within days), so RB attests only the digest observed at capture. Reserved for the podvm kinds.
2228	+
2229	Future signing is reserved via cosign or the OCI referrers API once GAR maturity is confirmed.
2230

file.content:
The provenance envelope carried by every
{rbtga_lode}
— one per Lode, batch-level — recording *capture-fidelity* rather than build-integrity.
The fetched-side parallel to the made-side
{rbtga_ark_vouch},
and a different claim: "we faithfully captured upstream X at digest D," not "we built this securely."
+
It has one canonical home: the in-GAR `:rbi_vouch` tag (the Lode self-describing in the registry, decoded by {rbtgo_lode_augur}). The host-side {rbf_fact_lode} carries only the bare touchmark, not the envelope — so reading host-side provenance costs a GAR call, the accepted price of a single canonical home.
+
Fields (schema `rbld-vouch-1`, near-term unsigned — `signature: null` — but schema-versioned):
`schema`, `kind`, `lode` (the touchmark), `acquired_at` (ISO-8601 UTC), `acquired_by` (the capturing Cloud Build service account), `capture_build` (the capture's own build id), `trust_grade`, and `members[]`.
Each `members[]` entry carries `name`, `origin`, `digest`, `verification`, and its assigned `tags` — so the captured identity and any Director-assigned names are themselves attested.
`members[]` is the cardinality axis: length 1 for singletons (bole), N for cohorts.
+
Two honest trust grades, declared per Lode (Palisade honesty — never over-claim):
+
* `verified-against-published` — bytes re-checkable against a persistent upstream (OCI content-address, or an out-of-band published checksum). The bole kind carries this grade.
* `recorded-at-acquisition` — upstream offers no durable re-checkable reference (e.g. quay rotates podvm images out within days), so RB attests only the digest observed at capture. Reserved for the podvm kinds.
+
Future signing is reserved via cosign or the OCI referrers API once GAR maturity is confirmed.

=== Foundry Fact Files

[[rbf_fact_hallmark]]
//axpof_fact
{rbf_fact_hallmark}::
Hallmark string.

[[rbf_fact_build_id]]
//axpof_fact
{rbf_fact_build_id}::
Cloud Build job ID.

[[rbf_fact_gar_root]]
//axpof_fact
{rbf_fact_gar_root}::
GAR registry root path.

[[rbf_fact_ark_stem]]
//axpof_fact

content.text: RBS0:2219 is the canonical field definition — the right home for the full sprue + schema bump + the per-member asymmetry note. Let me fix it and the four one-line stragglers (RBSLU, RBSLC, RBSLE, RBSHR).

input.old_string:
Fields (schema `rbld-vouch-1`, near-term unsigned — `signature: null` — but schema-versioned):
`schema`, `kind`, `lode` (the touchmark), `acquired_at` (ISO-8601 UTC), `acquired_by` (the capturing Cloud Build service account), `capture_build` (the capture's own build id), `trust_grade`, and `members[]`.
Each `members[]` entry carries `name`, `origin`, `digest`, `verification`, and its assigned `tags` — so the captured identity and any Director-assigned names are themselves attested.
`members[]` is the cardinality axis: length 1 for singletons (bole), N for cohorts.
input.new_string:
Fields (schema `rbld-vouch-2`, every key carrying the `rblv_` sprue, near-term unsigned — `rblv_signature: null` — but schema-versioned):
`rblv_schema`, `rblv_kind`, `rblv_lode` (the touchmark), `rblv_acquired_at` (ISO-8601 UTC — when the envelope was authored), `rblv_acquired_by` (the capturing Cloud Build service account), `rblv_capture_build` (the capture's own build id), `rblv_trust_grade`, and `rblv_members[]`.
Each `rblv_members[]` entry carries `rblv_name`, `rblv_origin`, `rblv_digest`, `rblv_verification`, and its assigned `rblv_tags` — so the captured identity and any Director-assigned names are themselves attested.
Kind-specific per-member fields ride on top of that common shape: the wsl kind adds `rblv_signing_key` (the gpg fingerprint), and the podvm kinds add per-member `rblv_acquired_at` / `rblv_capture_build` — present only because podvm refreshes, and so only it accumulates members across more than one authoring event; the per-member time then sits earlier than the envelope-level one, self-disclosing the authored-versus-captured split. The asymmetry tracks refresh-capability — a uniformity sweep must not graft these onto the single-capture kinds.
`rblv_members[]` is the cardinality axis: length 1 for singletons (bole), N for cohorts.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
//axhob_operation
{rbtgo_lode_underpin}

NOTE: This operation captures a vendor-published WSL rootfs into a single
{rbtga_lode}
— one
{gar_service}
package under the
{gar_lodes_namespace}
namespace, identified by a host-minted
{rbtga_touchmark}.
It is the wsl kind: a project-controlled Linux rootfs tarball, captured as the
substrate seed a Windows workload's `wsl --import` consumes.
It is the structural outlier among the Lode kinds — its upstream is an HTTPS
tarball plus an out-of-band published checksum, NOT an OCI registry image — so it
reuses neither the skopeo (bole) nor the docker (reliquary) registry-pull steps.

NOTE: Intent is declarative — no FQIN, no pinned digest.
Underpin takes the substrate version as two arguments (release + point, e.g.
`24.04 4`); the
{rbtr_director}
's workstation assembles the resolved tarball URL from a path-convention template,
and the cloud step DISCOVERS the checksum at capture time.
No full URL and no digest live in project config — per the no-FQIN principle, the
declaration stays intent and the pipeline computes the coordinate.

NOTE: Acquisition runs cloud-side.
The
{rbtr_director}
submits a
{gcb_service}
job on the tethered (egress-permitted) pool, executed by the
{rbtr_mason}
service account via the metadata server.
The workstation assembles the URL but never fetches the rootfs bytes — and never
performs the signature check, which is security-critical and so belongs in the
trusted GCP environment, not on a potentially-compromised station.

NOTE: Underpin verifies against the vendor's published, GPG-signed checksum.
The cloud step fetches the published `SHA256SUMS` and its detached signature,
fetches the signing key BY a pinned fingerprint into a clean keyring (so the
keyserver is not trusted — only a signature from exactly that key passes),
verifies the signature, extracts the expected digest for the target filename, then
verifies the fetched rootfs bytes against it.
This is the `verified-against-published` trust grade in its strongest form: the
vendor publishes and signs the checksum; RB pins only the signing key.

NOTE: Underpin bootstraps from no reliquary.
Its acquisition tool is curl over HTTPS (with gnupg and docker buildx, all present
on the Google-hosted `gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker` builder), not a registry pull,
so it needs no reliquary tool cohort.
The opaque tarball is wrapped as a single-layer OCI member by `FROM scratch` +
`COPY` (never `ADD`, which would extract and destroy the opaque blob).

NOTE: Underpin is capture-pure.
It mints the Lode, emits the
{rbtga_lode_vouch}
provenance envelope, and reports the touchmark — but writes NO consumer config.
Consumption (a host's `wsl --import` of the captured seed, and the nameplate
election that pins which touchmark a node provisions) is a separate, deferred
consumption-side layer that reads the
{rbf_fact_lode}
this operation writes; it is not part of underpin.

The
{rbtr_director}
captures a vendor rootfs into a Lode:

//axhos_step
. *Authenticate as
{rbtr_director}*:
.. Execute
{rbtoe_director_authenticate}
.. {rbbc_store}
`«DIRECTOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls

//axhos_step
. *Assemble the resolved URL (host-side)*:
.. {rbbc_store}
`«URL»` by formatting the version arguments (release, release.point, arch) into
the cdimage path-convention template — the only resolution the workstation does
.. The template, the default architecture, and the pinned signing-key fingerprint
are RB constants; the version is the operator's (later, a nameplate's) declarative choice

//axhos_step
. *Mint Touchmark (host-side)*:
.. {rbbc_store}
`«TOUCHMARK»` as the wsl kind-letter `w` + `YYMMDDHHMMSS` from current time
.. The host owns the stamp so the touchmark — and thus the
{rbf_fact_lode}
path — is known before the build
.. Example: `w260608120000`

//axhos_step
. *Submit Two-Step Capture Cloud Build*:
.. Compose `builds.create` JSON with:
... Step 1 — the Google-hosted docker builder image, script `rbgjl04-underpin-capture.sh` (the curl + GPG-verify + opaque-blob-wrap capture step)
... Step 2 — the Google-hosted docker builder image, script `rbgjl02-assemble-push-vouch.sh` (the vouch push)
... substitutions carrying GAR host/path, the
{gar_lodes_namespace}
root, `«TOUCHMARK»`, the rootfs member tag, the acquiring SA email, the trust grade, the envelope schema, the assembled `«URL»`, and the pinned signing-key fingerprint
... serviceAccount:
{rbtr_mason}
... pool: tethered (upstream fetch requires public egress)
.. {rbbc_submit}
link:https://cloud.google.com/build/docs/api/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.builds/create[builds.create] with:
... project:
{rbtgi_depot_project}
... location:
{rbrd_gcp_region}
.. {rbbc_store}
`«BUILD_ID»` from response `.metadata.build.id`
.. {rbbc_show}
build dispatched with `«BUILD_ID»` and the Cloud Console URL

//axhos_step
. *Capture Step (in-pool — rbgjl04)*:
.. Derive the sibling `SHA256SUMS` / `SHA256SUMS.gpg` URLs and the target filename from `«URL»`
.. Fetch the published checksums + detached signature; receive the signing key by the pinned fingerprint into a clean keyring; verify the signature; extract the expected digest for the target filename
.. Fetch the rootfs tarball (`curl`) and verify its bytes against that GPG-verified published digest; fail loud on mismatch
.. Wrap the opaque tarball as a single-layer OCI member (`FROM scratch` + `COPY`) and push it into the one Lode package under the clean member tag `:rbi_rootfs` — no digest or fingerprint layer
.. Author the
{rbtga_lode_vouch}
envelope JSON (schema `rbld-vouch-1`, trust grade `verified-against-published`, `members[]` one element — the cardinality axis — carrying `verification` `gpg-sha256-published` and the signing-key fingerprint) and stage it on the {rbtgr_workspace}
.. Write the result JSON (carrying the host-minted touchmark) to `buildStepOutputs`

//axhos_step
. *Vouch Step (in-pool — rbgjl02)*:
.. For the staged envelope, build a `FROM scratch` image copying `vouch.json` and push it to the Lode package under the `:rbi_vouch` tag — a distinct manifest sharing the one package

//axhos_step
. *Wait for Build Completion*:
.. {rbbc_poll}
link:https://cloud.google.com/build/docs/api/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.builds/get[cloudbuild.builds.get] for `«BUILD_ID»`
.. {rbbc_require}
final status is `SUCCESS`

//axhoot_typed_output
{rbf_fact_lode}
. *Write Capture-Facts*:
.. Read `buildStepOutputs[0]` (base64-decoded JSON) from the completed build to recover the host-minted `«TOUCHMARK»`
.. {rbbc_store}
the touchmark value and the wsl kind-brand enum as the two bare single-form facts of the
{rbf_fact_lode}
— carrying no provenance content; the envelope is the in-GAR `:rbi_vouch` tag, written cloud-side by the vouch step

//axhoc_completion
Successful execution captures the vendor rootfs into a single
{gar_service}
package at `{gar_lodes_namespace}/«TOUCHMARK»`, carrying the opaque rootfs blob
under its `:rbi_rootfs` member tag plus the `:rbi_vouch`
{rbtga_lode_vouch}
envelope, and hands the touchmark forward host-side as the
{rbf_fact_lode}.
+
The operation is capture-pure: no vessel or nameplate config is written.
The captured seed is a Director-dictated version closure — the substrate bytes are
exactly the published, signature-verified rootfs, never "whatever the operator had
installed".
+
Consumption is deferred this heat: underpin stops at the registry.
A host's `wsl --import` of the captured seed, and the nameplate election that pins
which touchmark a node provisions, are a separate consumption-side layer that
reads the capture-file — so election retries independently and survives, and a
one-to-many fan-out across nodes iterates by reading the file rather than by a verb
taking N node arguments.

lines: ... *Single-platform capture is conclave-only and load-bearing.* The cohort is consumed solely as GCB step images on amd64 workers, so other platforms are dead bytes — and capturing the full multi-arch index would make the Lode package a parent-index / child-manifest web: GAR refuses to delete a child manifest while its parent index still exists (`FAILED_PRECONDITION`, "manifest is referenced by parent manifests"), so a single `packages delete` of such a web removes nothing and banish must delete by convergence over multiple rounds (the cloud-dispatched delete handles this — see RBSLB and RBSCB; also link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/repositories/cleanup-policy[GAR cleanup policy] — child images referenced by a parent manifest are not deletable until the parent is removed). bole and wsl keep full-fidelity capture. This `--platform` flag must not propagate to other Lode kinds.

Do NOT use `cd`. Do NOT git commit. When done, build via `tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh` (run directly, never piped; then read ../logs-buk/last.txt) and report.

BACKGROUND: `rbld_immure` (Tools/rbk/rbldv_Immure.sh) now emits this diagnostic line right after resolving the family, BEFORE it loads any credential:

So invoking the immure colophon with a valid brand prints the resolved mapping and THEN dies at the credential-load step (no GCP creds in the fast tier). The fixture asserts the printed mapping for both brands.

  podvm-wsl    → `podvm-wsl -> quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl (kind vw)`

content.text:
Clean build. Here is the full report:

---

**What was done**

Three files edited; no new files created.

**1. `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrm_manifest.rs`**

Added the fixture name const (after `RBTDRM_FIXTURE_FOUNDRY_PATH`):
```rust
pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_PODVM_RESOLVE: &str = "podvm-resolve";
```

Added to `rbtdrm_required_colophons`:
```rust
RBTDRM_FIXTURE_PODVM_RESOLVE => Some(&[
    RBTDGC_IMMURE_PODVM,
]),
```

**2. `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrf_fast.rs`**

Added `RBTDGC_IMMURE_PODVM` to the `rbtdgc_consts` import block and `RBTDRM_FIXTURE_PODVM_RESOLVE` to the `rbtdrm_manifest` import block.

Added helper `rbtdrf_pr_invoke` (invokes the immure colophon, returns combined stdout+stderr regardless of exit code), case function `rbtdrf_podvm_resolve` (asserts both brand mapping substrings), case array `RBTDRF_CASES_PODVM_RESOLVE`, and fixture static `RBTDRF_FIXTURE_PODVM_RESOLVE`. The case invokes `RBTDGC_IMMURE_PODVM` (`rbw-lI`) with `["podvm-native", "5.6"]`, asserts combined output contains `"podvm-native -> quay.io/podman/machine-os (kind vn)"`, then repeats with `["podvm-wsl", "5.6"]` and asserts `"podvm-wsl -> quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl (kind vw)"`. Non-zero exit is not a verdict failure.

**3. `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs`**

Added `&crate::rbtdrf_fast::RBTDRF_FIXTURE_PODVM_RESOLVE,` immediately after `RBTDRF_FIXTURE_REGIME_SMOKE` in all 7 locations:

| Line (after edit) | Location |
|---|---|

content.text:
Confirmed the footgun is real: `rbtdri_invoke_impl` runs the tabtarget against the **real working regime** (it isolates only BURV temp/output dirs, not creds). The existing fast fixtures never exposed this because none invoke a GCP-capable colophon — `podvm-resolve` is the first. So on a credentialed workstation, running the fast suite would drive a **live native multi-GB capture**. I have not run the fast suite, and won't until this is resolved.

Here's where everything stands.

## Implementation complete — static checks green
- **Refresh core (mine):** `rbgjl07` carries the `rblv_`/`rbls_` sprue + the add-only merge (present members spliced, never re-resolved) + podvm-only per-member times. `rbldv` has the `--refresh <touchmark>` interface (no version arg — derived from the envelope, can't bism a version bump), the host present-set via augur, and **honest** orphan recovery (real digest from the tag→version ref, `acquired_at` from the version createTime — replacing the agent's `"unknown"` stubs).
- **Convergence fix (mine):** `rbgjl08`/`rbgjl09` guards changed `-s`→`-f` so an all-preserved refresh's empty selection no-ops instead of dying — without this, both convergence-after-crash and the recurring wsl refresh would crash.
- **Sprue sweep (delegated, verified):** the other three authors, augur's jq paths (display labels untouched), the body extracts, and ACG's ACGm_108 second worked application. I caught and fixed one bare straggler the sweep left — wsl's per-member `signing_key` → `rblv_signing_key`.
- **Specs (mine):** RBS0's canonical field definition now carries the sprue, the schema bump, and the per-member-asymmetry + dual-position legend; RBSLI documents refresh mode; RBSLA/RBSLU/RBSLC/RBSLE/RBSHR swept for `rbld-vouch-2`.
- **Service fixture (mine):** podvm-lifecycle gains a refresh step proving the all-preserved/convergent path on wsl (the existing augur now doubles as the preserve-originals check).
- **Verified:** shellcheck 212 clean, theurge builds clean, `rbgjl07` py-compiles. The refresh path's *live* correctness (the jq present-set, the GCB submit) is necessarily unverified until the live run — that's what the gate is for.

## One decision blocks — the fast fixture's safety
The fast `podvm-resolve` case asserts the resolve-diagnostic that prints *before* the credential load. Safe only if no creds are present — a footgun on a dev workstation. The clean fix is a **`buorb_immure_resolve_only` test-seam** (the sanctioned `buo` channel): `rbld_immure` short-circuits right after the diagnostic when that tweak is set, so the fast case never reaches GCP regardless of ambient creds. That makes it genuinely "pure bash, host-side" — which I read as *fulfilling* Fable's intent, since Fable's "no test seam" was explicitly scoped to the service wsl fixture's selection, not the fast resolve test.

My recommendation: add that seam. Alternatives in a line — **drop the fast case** (resolve_family is a trivial case-statement; the recurring wsl + one-time native gate already exercise dispatch), or **accept credless-only** (document it, keep the footgun).

Add the `buo` seam? And once the fast fixture is safe, do you want me to run the fast suite, then hand you the live verification plan (the wsl refresh service run + the one-time 8-leaf native gate) for your go — or run the fast suite only and hold there?

content: Pace ₢BHAAL (podvm immure refresh + envelope sprue) is implemented and passing static checks. The one open decision: whether to add a `buorb_immure_resolve_only` test-seam so the fast fixture can't trigger a live capture, before I run the fast suite.

message.content:
For ₢BHAAL (from Fable): The footgun is real and well caught — I confirmed independently: the diagnostic
  prints at resolve, credentials load right after, and theurge isolates only BURV dirs. This was a
  passing-fast-suite-that-spends-money failure mode, the worst kind. Good stop.

  Add the seam — blessed, with precedent. buorb_ensconce_stamp already lives in rbtdrc_crucible.rs, so
  buorb_immure_resolve_only is the second use of an established theurge pattern, not new plumbing. And your
  scoping read of my "no test seam" is exactly right — that constraint was about the service fixture's selection
  diverging from production constants; a resolve-only short-circuit changes no shipped value. Reject the other
  two alternatives explicitly: credless-only documents a footgun instead of removing it, and dropping the case
  forfeits the one thing it exists for — the native arm's tuple values (family ref, selection constant) are the
  post-gate rot surface, and "it's a trivial case statement" is not the point.

  Three refinements as you build it:

  1. The short-circuit announces itself loudly — print a line naming the tweak (immure: short-circuit after
  resolve (buorb_immure_resolve_only)) before exiting success. A transcript reader must see why immure stopped
  early; a silent early exit reads as a bug.
  2. The fast case asserts that marker too, not just the resolve diagnostic — so a silently-broken seam (tweak
  ignored, build fired) at least fails the case rather than passing while money burns.
  3. Don't solve the structural problem here. The deeper finding — fast-tier credlessness is convention, not
  construction, and the first GCP-capable colophon in fast nearly fired a live build — is recorded as
  Memos/memo-20260610-heat-BH-fast-tier-credless-by-convention.md for the terminal triage pace. Your seam fixes
  this instance; the memo owns the class.

  Run plan: fast suite once the seam lands, then hold. Hand the live verification plan (the wsl refresh service
  run + the one-time 8-leaf native gate) to the operator for an explicit go — cloud spend stays operator-gated,
  per the heat's standing posture.

content.text: Blessed with precedent and three refinements. Let me study the `buorb_ensconce_stamp` pattern first so my seam matches the established shape on both the bash side and the theurge side.

content.text: The precedent is clear — `buorb_ensconce_stamp` checks `BURE_TWEAK_NAME` in bash and theurge passes it via `extra_env`. Let me build the seam the same way. First the bash short-circuit, placed right after the resolve diagnostic and before any credential load, announcing itself loudly:

input.old_string:
# Resolved-tuple diagnostic, emitted before any credential load so a host-side
  # (no-GCB) test can assert the family mapping for either brand by reading this line.
  buc_info "Immure family resolved: ${z_brand} -> ${z_quay_family} (kind ${z_kind}), leaves: ${z_selection}"

  buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
input.new_string:
# Resolved-tuple diagnostic, emitted before any credential load so a host-side
  # (no-GCB) test can assert the family mapping for either brand by reading this line.
  buc_info "Immure family resolved: ${z_brand} -> ${z_quay_family} (kind ${z_kind}), leaves: ${z_selection}"

  # Resolve-only test seam (buo sprue; BURE enforces the shape). The fast tier asserts
  # the family mapping host-side, but immure is GCP-capable: without this short-circuit
  # a credentialed workstation would load creds and fire a live build right below. When
  # the tweak is set, stop here — AFTER the diagnostic, BEFORE any credential or network
  # touch — and announce loudly, so a transcript reader sees why immure stopped early
  # (a silent early exit reads as a bug). Mirrors rbldb_Bole's buorb_ensconce_stamp.

input.content:
# Fast-tier credlessness is convention, not construction (heat ₣BH finding)

*Captured 2026-06-10 during the podvm platform-fanout pace. For the terminal triage
pace: the seam below fixes the instance; this memo owns the class.*

## The near-miss

The fast suite's first **GCP-capable** fixture (`podvm-resolve`, asserting
`zrbld_immure_resolve_family` maps both quay brands) was authored to invoke the immure
colophon for real and assert the family-resolution diagnostic. The diagnostic prints
*before* immure loads credentials — so on a **credless** host the invocation fast-fails
right after it, and the assertion holds.

But theurge's invocation path (`rbtdri_invoke_impl`, `rbtdri_invocation.rs`) isolates
only the BURV output/temp directories — **not** the regime or the credential files. It
runs the real tabtarget against the operator's real working regime. So on a
**credentialed** workstation, the same fast fixture would sail past the diagnostic,
load Director credentials, and **submit a live multi-GB Cloud Build** — a
passing-fast-suite-that-spends-money failure, surfacing only as cloud spend.

Every prior fast fixture (regime-validation, enrollment-validation, regime-smoke,
dockerfile-hygiene, recipe-validation, foundry-path) invokes only config/local
colophons, so none ever depended on credlessness. The property "the fast tier is
credless" was therefore **never enforced — only never tested**. `podvm-resolve` is the
first fixture for which the gap bites.

## The instance fix (landed this pace)

A resolve-only test seam, `buorb_immure_resolve_only` (the sanctioned `buo` channel;
second use of the theurge tweak pattern after `buorb_ensconce_stamp`). `rbld_immure`
short-circuits to a clean exit 0 immediately after the resolve diagnostic — before any
credential or network touch — and announces itself loudly. The fast case passes the
tweak via `extra_env` and asserts **both** the family mapping and the short-circuit
marker, so a silently-ignored seam (build fired) fails the case rather than passing.

This makes `podvm-resolve` safe regardless of ambient credentials. It does **not** fix
the class.

## The class (for triage)

Fast-tier credlessness remains a property of *which colophons the fixtures happen to
invoke*, not a guarantee the harness enforces. A future GCP-capable fast fixture that
forgets its seam re-opens the same footgun, silently.

Options to consider when the class is addressed (not prescriptive):

- **Enforce credlessness by construction** — run fast-tier invocations under a regime
  whose `RBDC_*_RBRA` paths are guaranteed absent (a blank/template regime), so any
  GCP-capable colophon fast-fails at credential load no matter what the fixture forgot.
- **Refuse GCP-capable colophons in the fast tier** — a harness-level guard (allowlist
  of fast-safe colophons, or a "this colophon touches GCP" annotation theurge checks),
  so the footgun fails at fixture-registration time, not at money-spend time.
- **Keep the per-fixture seam discipline** — accept that GCP-capable fast fixtures must
  carry a resolve/dry seam, and document it as a standing rule with a registration-time
  check.

The first removes the footgun; the second removes the whole hazard class; the third
keeps the current convention but makes it explicit. The choice is the triage pace's.

# Fast-tier credlessness is convention, not construction — near-miss record

Date: 2026-06-10
Status: standing-hazard record from the podvm refresh pace; the immediate instance was caught
pre-run and fixed with a buo test-seam. Triage material for the ₣BH terminal memo-walk pace.

## The near-miss

The fast-tier podvm resolve case invokes the real `rbw-lI` tabtarget against the real working
regime — theurge isolates only BURV temp/output dirs, never credentials. On a credentialed
workstation (the operator's always is), the case would have sailed past its asserted diagnostic
into a live multi-GB Cloud Build submission, inside the `fast` suite, on every run.
Caught by inspection before any run; fixed by short-circuiting the verb under a
`buorb_immure_resolve_only` tweak.

## The standing hazard

Nothing structural makes the fast tier credless. Every existing fast fixture is safe only
because none happened to invoke a GCP-capable colophon — the first one that did nearly fired a
live build. Future fast cases can repeat this silently: the failure mode is a *passing* fast
suite that spends money and mutates the depot.

## Triage question

Whether to enforce fast-tier credlessness by construction. Candidate shapes (not designed, just
recorded): the fast suite runner exports a poison marker that the credential-load path refuses
(loud "fast tier reached a credential load" die); or theurge's fast tier asserts no network
egress; or a per-case declaration ("reaches GCP: yes/no") validated against the tier. Each has
costs; the buo-seam-per-case convention may also be judged sufficient — but then the convention
needs stating somewhere an author of a new fast case will read it.

content.text:
Fast suite green: **152 passed, 0 failed** across 10 fixtures — and critically `rbtdrf_podvm_resolve` **passed in seconds**, which confirms the seam fired (immure short-circuited under the tweak), both brand mappings resolved correctly, the marker asserted, and **no build fired**. The footgun is disarmed and the native-arm regression cover is live.

**To your question: no new issues — but yes, there's more testing, and I'd advise against wrapping until it runs or you defer it.** Everything verified so far is static + host-side. The refresh path's *live* GCB behavior is unverified — that's exactly the operator-gated cloud gate Fable's run plan reserved for your explicit go. Here's what's actually still dark:

**The live verification gate (cloud spend — your call):**
1. **Service wsl refresh proof** — `tt/rbw-tf.FixtureRun.sh podvm-lifecycle`. Proves the refresh *machinery* end-to-end against live GAR: fresh 2-leaf wsl capture → **refresh (all-preserved: host present-set via augur, select splice, the empty-selection no-op through the rbgjl08/09 `-f` fix, vouch re-push)** → augur preserve-check → per-member jettison → banish → restored. Modest cost (wsl leaves, not the multi-GB native). This is the one I'd run first — it exercises the whole common refresh path.
2. **One-time native 8-leaf gate** — a live `rbw-lI podvm-native 5.6`, then augur + banish. Proves native fan-out + full curation. Multi-GB, run once, recorded in the wrap, never in the suite.

**Two honest gaps even after those run:**
- **Orphan recovery is inspection-only.** It triggers solely after a *crashed* refresh (tags cp'd but un-enveloped), which no fixture injects — it's covered by the convergence logic and review, not a live test. Flagging so it's a known, not a surprise.
- The all-preserved refresh's jq present-set computation gets its first live exercise in gate #1 — shellcheck can't validate jq logic, so #1 is its real proof.

So: want me to run gate #1 now (and #2 after, if it's clean)? Or defer the live gate to a deliberate session and hold the wrap until then? I won't wrap or spend until you say.
